maps.area: THE ALLOCATION SURFACE — MemorySpace, the single shared alloc space behind Runtime, GC-trigger plumbing, and every allocation site class (interpreter opcodes, RTS helpers, JIT bridge, whole-region JIT)

assumptions.what: The bump pointer `self.used` is a plain non-atomic usize mutated through `&mut self`; read-check-write (`let length_idx = self.used; ... self.used = new_used`) is assumed exclusive.
assumptions.breaks_because: Two simultaneous mutators bump-allocating would both read the same `used`, both commit, and return OVERLAPPING object pointers — instant heap corruption. Today exclusivity is provided only by the giant lock; with N mutators the counter must become per-thread (each thread owns its nursery's `used`) or atomic (contended, slow).

assumptions.what: `HeapCell(UnsafeCell<Option<MemorySpace>>)` with hand-written `unsafe impl Send/Sync`; `heap_mut(&self) -> &mut Option<MemorySpace>` hands out a `&mut` to ANY caller under the contract 'the giant lock IS the heap's mutual exclusion' (only the lock-holder or the barrier-satisfied collector dereferences).
assumptions.breaks_because: With two threads executing bytecode simultaneously, two live `&mut MemorySpace` exist at once — undefined behavior by Rust aliasing rules and a real data race on `used`/`storage`. This is THE central single-heap assumption; every other alloc path funnels through it.

assumptions.what: 'Only valid while this thread holds ML memory ... the giant lock is the heap's mutual exclusion, so this is the sole live &mut MemorySpace.' Every interpreter/RTS/JIT allocation resolves through this one accessor to the one shared space.
assumptions.breaks_because: N simultaneous mutators calling this concurrently each get an aliasing `&mut` to the same space. Per-thread nurseries fix it by making this resolve to a field the Interpreter OWNS (note mod.rs:522-529: the heap was deliberately MOVED from a per-Interpreter field into runtime.heap_mut() in increment 3b — breaking the giant lock partially reverses that move for the fast path).

assumptions.what: Every step reads `alloc_space_ref().used_words()` — the SHARED space's counter — and compares against the per-thread `gc_trigger_words` mirror (mod.rs:561). Assumes the reader is the only writer of `used`.
assumptions.breaks_because: With N mutators, peers write `used` concurrently → data race on a plain usize read every step. Also semantically: multiple threads can cross the threshold in the same instant and all call request_gc_collect. Per-nursery, the hot check becomes a race-free read of the thread's OWN nursery counter, and global-pressure checks move to the nursery-refill slow path.

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-runtime/src/sched.rs:768-791 (Runtime::request_gc doc: 'The caller is THE running thread (only the running thread allocates, so only it can hit the trigger)')
assumptions.what: GC-request arbitration assumes exactly one allocator exists, so exactly one thread can ever request; `gc_requested: bool` under the scheduler Mutex has a single setter.
assumptions.breaks_because: With N allocating mutators, several threads hit their triggers concurrently → need collector election (first setter wins, losers park at the barrier instead of double-collecting) and the barrier predicate must tolerate a requester that is itself asked to park.

assumptions.what: The collector takes ONE `&mut MemorySpace` (mod.rs:2939 `runtime.heap_mut().as_mut()?`), and Cheney assumes ONE contiguous from-space: a single `from_start..from_end` range (gc.rs:304-305), one linear `from_objects` pre-pass walk (gc.rs:311-334), one same-capacity scratch to-space (gc.rs:337-338), one `replace_storage` swap (gc.rs:388, 413-417).
assumptions.breaks_because: With per-thread nurseries there are N+1 from-spaces. The `is this word a from-space pointer?` predicate becomes multi-range set membership; the object pre-pass must walk every nursery; the swap/reset must run per space. Crucially there is NO write barrier, so tenured/image objects (and peer nurseries) freely point into any nursery — a nursery-local collection is unsound; every collection must remain global STW over the union.

assumptions.what: 'Exhaustion is TERMINAL BY DESIGN — callers must NOT try GC-then-retry: RTS helpers like do_alloc_ref and ALLOC_WORD_MEMORY cache the allocation pointer across the call.' I.e., ALLOCATION NEVER COLLECTS; GC fires only at step boundaries. Dozens of sites hold raw `*mut PolyWord` and heap PolyWords in Rust locals across allocations relying on this.
assumptions.breaks_because: The naive per-thread-nursery response to 'nursery full' is 'collect now' — which silently converts EVERY multi-alloc raw-pointer sequence into a use-after-free (Rust locals are not GC roots). Examples: make_syserr_exception (rts.rs:5713-5735, pair then packet), the sysword/SOME/pair/packet 4-alloc chain (rts.rs:5795-5830), select's per-set vector + 3-tuple (rts.rs:3281-3298), box_f32_tagged's two allocs (rts.rs:5111-5122), bigint results, ALLOC_WORD_MEMORY's popped `init` held in a local across allocate (mod.rs:4861-4874), do_tuple's popped values (mod.rs:6838-6848). The invariant must be PRESERVED: nursery exhaustion must fetch a fresh chunk, never collect in-line.

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-runtime/src/interpreter/mod.rs:7684-7712 + rts.rs:224-266 (rts_call builds RtsContext { alloc_space: self.alloc_space_mut() }, then park_while_blocking releases the giant lock)
assumptions.what: An RTS function holds `Option<&'a mut MemorySpace>` (rts.rs:146) for its whole body — including ACROSS a blocking park that releases the giant lock — justified as 'the ctx stays dormant across the window'. The peer collector then takes ANOTHER `&mut` to the same space (mod.rs:2939) and may replace_storage it while the parked `&mut` is live.
assumptions.breaks_because: Already aliasing-UB-adjacent today; with simultaneous mutators a parked thread's ctx alias coexists with N active `&mut`s. Per-thread nurseries fix mutator-vs-mutator aliasing (each ctx borrows its OWN nursery) but the GC-vs-parked-nursery contract must be made explicit: the collector must evacuate the parked thread's nursery contents (reaching it via the registry/published roots, like parked_roots) without invalidating the `&mut MemorySpace` the parked RtsContext still holds — i.e. mutate through the same struct, never replace the struct, and ideally change RtsContext.alloc_space to a re-derived raw handle rather than a long-lived `&mut`.

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-runtime/src/rts.rs — 25 alloc_or_exit call sites across ~16 shared helpers: alloc_pair (2277), make_sysword (3097), select vectors/triple (3281-3298), cons cell (3432), bigint_to_poly_word (3781), fd/stream wrappers (4340, 4383, 4469), box_f32_tagged (4972→5111-5122), box_real (5087), alloc_thread_object_stub (5155), make_simple_exception (5687), make_syserr_exception (5713), syserr+sysword chain (5795-5826), make_exception (6061, feeding make_overflow/interrupt/div_exception 6083-6100), alloc_poly_string (6385), string builder (6442), alloc_empty_string (6472), byte cell (6488)
assumptions.what: All RTS allocation reaches the heap ONLY via `ctx.alloc_space: Option<&mut MemorySpace>` seeded by the dispatch site from the one shared space; helpers assume `alloc_or_exit` never moves prior allocations within the same call.
assumptions.breaks_because: With N mutators the seed must become the CALLING THREAD's nursery. The `RtsContext` shape can survive unchanged (the funnel is the fix-once point), but the exit(4) in alloc_or_exit (space.rs:119-129) now kills N threads mid-flight, and its 'used/capacity' diagnostic describes one nursery, not the heap.

assumptions.what: JIT trampolines reach the heap via the per-OS-thread JIT_INTERP thread-local `*mut Interpreter` → jit_alloc_space_mut (mod.rs:1482-1493, 'sound because the caller runs while this thread holds ML memory and is the sole heap accessor — the giant-lock discipline'). They call `space.try_alloc` DIRECTLY, bypassing Interpreter::allocate, and return None on exhaustion.
assumptions.breaks_because: The thread-local per-thread routing already works for N threads, but all four resolve to the ONE shared space; and because they bypass the allocate() funnel, a nursery-chunk slow path added only to `allocate` would leave these returning failure when the nursery empties even though the pool has room — they must be re-funneled through the same nursery-alloc entry point.

assumptions.what: The whole-region JIT bakes the RAW ADDRESS of `MemorySpace.used` (`addr_of!(s.used)`) plus a scalar trigger into the region's ExnCtx for the inline back-edge GC poll; assumed stable because 'the heap is a fixed Box inside the Runtime' and replace_storage swaps the Box's CONTENTS, never the MemorySpace struct itself.
assumptions.breaks_because: With per-thread nurseries the ctx must carry the DISPATCHING thread's nursery counter; if nurseries live in a growable Vec, Vec reallocation moves the MemorySpace structs and every baked `&used` dangles (nurseries must be Box-pinned); and a mid-region nursery-chunk swap changes WHICH counter is live, leaving the baked pointer reading a retired chunk — the poll silently never trips (missed safepoints → a compute-bound region stalls the STW barrier).

assumptions.what: Heap attachment happens once, single-threaded, at build time via `Arc::get_mut(&mut self.runtime)`; a forked child gets NO heap provisioning of its own — just a copied `gc_trigger_words` scalar — because it shares the one space. test_set_gc_trigger_words (mod.rs:3611-3616) likewise silently no-ops on the shared trigger once the Arc has >1 ref.
assumptions.breaks_because: With per-thread nurseries, fork must provision a nursery at RUNTIME (grab a chunk from the global pool before the child's first alloc opcode), and thread exit must retire/return it (or short-lived threads leak the pool dry). The build-time-only Arc::get_mut pattern cannot express any post-build shared-trigger update.

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-runtime/src/interpreter/mod.rs:1715-1730 (alloc_space_range, untrusted-mode predicate) + rts.rs:343-376 (RtsSafeSpaces live alloc range) + mod.rs:3193-3210 (POLYML_GC_AUDIT walk)
assumptions.what: The typed-deref predicate, the RTS safe-space snapshot, and the GC audit all read THE one live alloc-space range ('read FRESH — the Cheney GC swaps the alloc space wholesale').
assumptions.breaks_because: With N nurseries, a perfectly legitimate pointer into a PEER thread's nursery (objects escape via shared refs — normal, there's no isolation) falls outside 'the' alloc range → --untrusted mode would produce false BadImage halts and the audit would miss residuals in peer nurseries. All three must enumerate every nursery + the shared space.

assumptions.what: The safepoint poll — the ONLY place a non-allocating thread notices a GC request — takes the full scheduler Mutex per poll (the fn is named 'relaxed' but is a lock acquisition), amortized over 65536 steps, acceptable because at most one peer ever contends.
assumptions.breaks_because: With N mutators polling AND a nursery-refill slow path that must also check gc_requested (a thread grabbing a chunk during a pending STW request must park, not race the collector for pool state), this Mutex becomes a real contention point and the request flag must become a genuine AtomicBool readable without the scheduler lock.

assumptions.what: The cached AtomicUsize is the env-var PERCENT (process-global, read-once) — NOT the trigger itself; `gc_trigger_words` is a plain usize computed once from the ONE space's capacity (mod.rs:1605-1608) and mirrored per-Interpreter and into Runtime (sched.rs:313, plain non-atomic field).
assumptions.breaks_because: The percent cache is already thread-safe, but the trigger derivation `cap * thresh / 100` is meaningless per-nursery (a cap/N nursery at 80% triggers N× too often); global accounting must be Σ(words handed out to nurseries) + tenured-used against the total budget — a new AtomicUsize maintained at chunk-grant/retire time, not a per-step read.

required_changes: Give each ThreadContext (Interpreter) an OWNED nursery: a Box-pinned MemorySpace field (partially reverting increment 3b's move of the heap into Runtime.heap_mut for the fast path). alloc_space_mut/alloc_space_ref (mod.rs:1500-1511) resolve to it; all 11 Interpreter::allocate callers, the 4 direct try_alloc sites (SIGNED/UNSIGNED_TO_LONG_W mod.rs:6335/6354, alloc_real 7329, alloc_lg_word 7419), the RtsContext seeding (mod.rs:7626/7703/7784/7805, rts.rs:3832), and the 4 jit_bridge dispatch fns inherit the change through the existing funnels without per-site edits.

Runtime owns a global chunk POOL (Mutex<Vec<Box<MemorySpace>>> or free-list of fixed-size chunks, e.g. 1-4 MB) plus the tenured space; replace HeapCell's single-space contract with: nursery = thread-owned, tenured + pool = lock-guarded. Fork provisions a child nursery from the pool before spawn; exit_running/exit_parked/Drop retire it.

PRESERVE the 'allocation never collects' invariant (space.rs:131-142): the nursery-exhaustion slow path grabs a fresh chunk from the pool under the pool lock — it must also check gc_requested there and park if a collection is pending. Objects larger than a chunk allocate directly in the shared space under lock. This keeps all ~40 raw-pointer-across-alloc sites (25 rts.rs alloc_or_exit sites, ALLOC_WORD_MEMORY/do_tuple locals, jit_bridge) sound without introducing upstream's SaveVec handle discipline.

GC-trigger accounting: per-step hot check becomes a read of the thread's OWN nursery counter (race-free, faster than today's shared-space deref); GLOBAL pressure = AtomicUsize of granted words, compared against capacity*threshold at chunk-grant time; crossing it sets gc_requested so the STW collection still happens at step-boundary safepoints via the existing request_gc barrier.

Make gc_requested a real AtomicBool (sched.rs:877-884 currently locks the scheduler Mutex per poll) and add collector election: N threads may request simultaneously; one becomes collector, losers park at the barrier.

Generalize gc::collect (gc.rs:300-390) to a MULTI-RANGE from-space: the from-space predicate becomes membership over {all nurseries} (∪ tenured when doing a full collect), the from_objects pre-pass walks each space, evacuation targets the tenured/to space, and each nursery is RESET (used=0) rather than storage-swapped — so a parked RtsContext's long-lived `&mut MemorySpace` alias never sees its struct replaced. Interpreter::gc (mod.rs:2867+) must reach every peer's nursery: publish a nursery pointer alongside parked_roots (same handle discipline) or store it on ThreadHandle.

Re-wire the whole-region JIT alloc/poll plumbing: ExnCtx.gc_used_ptr/gc_trigger (region.rs:197-202, boundary.rs:1555-1576) derived per-dispatch from the dispatching thread's Box-pinned nursery; define the chunk-swap story (either forbid chunk swap inside a region — region_alloc falls back to the interpreter path — or re-derive the pointer after every region_alloc return). Flag-off code must still compile against the new signatures.

Extend the untrusted-mode predicate (alloc_space_range mod.rs:1715-1730), RtsSafeSpaces' live-alloc range (rts.rs rts_safe_spaces snapshot), and the POLYML_GC_AUDIT walk (mod.rs:3125-3210) + audit thunks to enumerate ALL nurseries + tenured — peer-nursery pointers are legal.

Fix heap-exhaustion semantics: HeapExhausted/alloc_or_exit messages must report Σ across nurseries + tenured vs the POLYML_HEAP_BYTES budget; pool exhaustion (not single-nursery exhaustion) is the terminal condition; alloc_or_exit's process::exit(4) should first coordinate (or be replaced by a raised SysErr where reachable) since it now kills N live threads.

Change RtsContext.alloc_space's lifetime discipline around parks: either re-derive the nursery reference after park_while re-acquires (a raw NurseryRef re-borrowed post-park) or document + enforce that the collector only mutates the parked nursery's CONTENTS in place. Add a fence test: peer GC fires while a thread is parked in accept/select, then the parked thread's post-park RTS allocation and its rts_park_root cell both land correctly.

Rebuild all warm checkpoints ONLY if the RTS register() order changes (it shouldn't for this work), but re-run tools/regression.sh fast + the concurrency_* suites; add a nursery-specific negative control (two threads allocating flat-out with a tiny pool, POLYML_GC_AUDIT=1) mirroring the H1/H2 teeth tests.
hazards: THE killer trap: introducing GC-at-allocation-time (the 'obvious' nursery-full response). Rust locals are not GC roots — every popped operand held across allocate() (ALLOC_WORD_MEMORY's init, do_tuple's values) and every multi-alloc RTS helper holding a raw *mut across a second alloc_or_exit (make_syserr_exception's pair→packet, the 4-alloc sysword chain, select's vectors→triple) becomes a silent use-after-free that only manifests under allocation pressure. The existing comments at space.rs:131-142 and mod.rs:442-447 are the contract; keep allocation GC-free via chunk handoff.

Nursery storage identity: if nurseries live in a Vec<MemorySpace>, Vec growth MOVES the structs — the whole-region JIT's baked addr_of!(space.used) pointers and any RtsContext `&mut` dangle even though no GC ran. Nurseries must be individually Box-pinned (or indexed through a stable arena).

Nursery-reset vs storage-swap: gc.rs replace_storage frees the old Box. A parked thread's RtsContext still holds `&mut MemorySpace` into that struct; today's Cheney swap on the shared space is survivable because the struct is stable, but doing per-nursery swaps while a park is in flight frees storage a blocked thread will bump into after re-acquire ONLY IF the reset happens before its re-acquire — the re-acquire ordering (park_while re-acquires before returning) is what makes reset-under-STW sound; any future async/timeout park path that touches the nursery before re-acquiring breaks it.

No write barrier exists: tenured→nursery and peer-nursery→nursery pointers are unrestricted, so ANY collection must stop the world and scan the union — a 'minor GC of just my nursery' is unsound as designed and must not be attempted without adding remembered sets first.

Multiple simultaneous trigger-crossers: two threads calling request_gc concurrently was structurally impossible (one allocator); after the change, the second requester must park as a peer, not double-collect — and a thread blocked on the POOL lock during a pending STW must count as parked for the barrier or the collector deadlocks (the pool lock becomes a new class of 'blocked but in_ml' state, exactly the shape H1/B4 was built to exclude).

The hot-path perf cliff: making the per-step trigger check read a shared atomic (instead of a thread-local nursery counter) or leaving gc_requested behind the scheduler Mutex at a finer poll cadence will eat the interpreter's ~92M steps/sec; the design must keep the per-step path free of shared-cacheline traffic.

jit_bridge's four alloc fns bypass Interpreter::allocate and call space.try_alloc directly — adding the chunk slow path only to allocate() leaves JIT'd code failing allocations (returning None → spurious raises) when the nursery empties; they must share the slow-path funnel.

Untrusted-mode false positives: peer-nursery pointers are legal data; forgetting to register all nurseries in SafeSpaces/alloc_space_range turns --untrusted into a randomly-failing mode only under real concurrency (nondeterministic BadImage halts — miserable to debug).

gc_trigger_words is mirrored in three places (Interpreter field, Runtime field, region ExnCtx scalar) — per-nursery redesign must not leave a stale mirror; the RTS-token-staleness lesson applies: stale scalar mirrors fail silently.

exit(4) in alloc_or_exit while holding the pool lock or giant lock: with N threads, a heap-exhaustion exit can now fire while peers are mid-STW-barrier; ensure the exit path doesn't strand a collector waiting on a thread that will never park.
maps.design_opinion: Adopt upstream's actual architecture in simplified form: fixed-size nursery CHUNKS handed to threads from a global pool, with the load-bearing local invariant — allocation NEVER collects — preserved intact. That invariant is this codebase's biggest deliberate divergence from upstream (which pays for alloc-time GC with the SaveVec handle discipline on every RTS allocation), and it is what keeps ~40 raw-pointer allocation sites sound; converting them to handles would be a large, error-prone rewrite for zero benefit, whereas chunk-handoff keeps every existing site correct by construction. The three existing funnels (Interpreter::allocate/alloc_space_mut, RtsContext.alloc_space, JIT_INTERP→jit_alloc_space_mut) mean per-thread nurseries are a fix-in-one-place change for the fast path; the real engineering is in the GC (multi-range from-space, reaching parked nurseries via the parked_roots discipline, collector election) and in the two pointer-identity traps (Box-pin nurseries; re-derive the region JIT's baked used-counter per dispatch). Keep the per-step trigger check a plain read of the thread's own nursery counter — it gets FASTER than today — and move all shared accounting to the chunk-grant slow path where a Mutex is amortized. Finally, extend the teeth-proven negative-control test pattern (H1/H2, GC-audit) to the nursery pool before trusting any of it: this codebase's own history says the concurrency test is the discovery instrument.

maps.area: GC: gc.rs Cheney collector, interpreter/mod.rs gc() orchestration + audit, collector election/handshake (sched.rs request_gc), cross-nursery pointer flows, export/snapshot path

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-runtime/src/gc.rs:65-96 (Collector.from_start/from_end, contains_polyword, contains_raw_ptr) and gc.rs:127-149 (forward_impl range check)
assumptions.what: From-space is exactly ONE contiguous [from_start, from_end) range; every membership test is a two-comparison range check against it.
assumptions.breaks_because: With N per-thread nurseries there are N disjoint from-space ranges. A pointer from thread A's stack (or an image-mutable slot) into thread B's nursery fails the single-range test, is silently left un-forwarded, and dangles the moment B's nursery storage is freed — a classic GC UAF. The membership test must become a sorted-disjoint-range-set lookup.

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-runtime/src/gc.rs:300-338 (collect signature `alloc: &mut MemorySpace` + the from_objects pre-pass at 311-334) and gc.rs:78 (from_objects table)
assumptions.what: There is exactly one space to collect, and its live region is a densely-packed sequence of well-formed objects starting at index 0 (the pre-pass walks [0, used_words) linearly to build the mid-object-pointer map).
assumptions.breaks_because: N nurseries need N linear walks and either a merged sorted object map or a per-space map selected by range. If any nursery is missed from the map, mid-object PC pointers into it (handler PCs / retPCs on any thread's stack, closure word-0 code pointers) either hit the untracked_addrs panic (gc.rs:381) or are silently dropped.

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-runtime/src/gc.rs:210-228 (bump_to overflow assert) + gc.rs:336-338 (`let cap = alloc.capacity_words(); scratch = vec![...; cap]`)
assumptions.what: To-space sized equal to the single from-space capacity, justified by 'a Cheney copy never produces more live words than from-space held'.
assumptions.breaks_because: Evacuating N from-spaces into one to-space can carry up to Σ(nursery live) words; sizing to-space as one nursery's capacity makes the release-time assert fire mid-collection (a panic with the heap half-tombstoned = unrecoverable). Sizing/targeting must become Σ capacities, or upstream-style promotion into shared local spaces.

assumptions.what: The scan loop walks a single to_storage buffer linearly with one scan index until it catches the single bump pointer.
assumptions.breaks_because: If evacuation targets multiple destination spaces (e.g. mutable/immutable split or per-thread to-nurseries, as upstream does), the fixpoint needs per-destination scan pointers and an outer loop until ALL are caught up; a single-buffer scan misses children copied into the second destination.

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-runtime/src/gc.rs:183-207 (forward_object tombstone install: plain read of header then plain write)
assumptions.what: Exactly one collector thread ever forwards; the tombstone check-then-install needs no atomicity.
assumptions.breaks_because: Only breaks if collection itself is parallelized (2+ GC workers): two workers racing on the same object would both copy it, producing two to-space copies and divergent forwarding. Upstream quick_gc.cpp uses InterlockedCompareExchange on the length word for exactly this. Fine to keep single-collector for the first cut — but this is the line that must become a CAS later.

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-runtime/src/sched.rs:288-296 (HeapCell = UnsafeCell<Option<MemorySpace>>) + sched.rs:347-350 (Runtime::heap_mut returning &mut) + space.rs:143-153 (try_alloc non-atomic used RMW)
assumptions.what: One shared heap whose mutual exclusion IS the giant lock; heap_mut hands out `&mut MemorySpace` on the promise that the caller is the sole lock-holder.
assumptions.breaks_because: Two simultaneous mutators calling heap_mut() create aliasing &mut — instant Rust UB before any GC even runs; try_alloc's `self.used` read-modify-write races, handing two threads the same body address. This is THE root assumption the mission breaks: allocation must become per-thread nurseries (unsynchronized because single-owner) plus shared spaces touched only under the STW protocol.

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-runtime/src/interpreter/mod.rs:2939 (`let alloc = unsafe { self.runtime.heap_mut() }.as_mut()?`) and mod.rs:2944-2946 (from_lo/from_hi single range)
assumptions.what: gc() collects THE one heap and remembers ONE [from_lo, from_hi) range for the post-collection audit.
assumptions.breaks_because: With N nurseries gc() must iterate a nursery registry (all of them are from-spaces every cycle — any subset-collection scheme needs remembered sets we don't have), and the audit's in_old predicate must test the union of N ranges or it goes blind to residuals pointing into the other N-1 freed nurseries.

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-runtime/src/interpreter/mod.rs:2935 (`let image_roots = self.image_mutable_roots.clone()`) vs mod.rs:1664-1667 (with_image_mutable_root pushes to BOTH self and runtime) and mod.rs:1334-1363 (fork ctx clones from runtime)
assumptions.what: The collector scans its OWN per-Interpreter copy of the image-mutable root list, assumed identical to the global set.
assumptions.breaks_because: The per-thread clone is a snapshot at Interpreter construction. If any thread registers a new image-mutable region after a peer's context was built, that peer-as-collector scans a stale list and misses roots → moved objects referenced from the unscanned region dangle. gc() must read Runtime.image_roots (the shared, locked list) instead.

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-runtime/src/sched.rs:791-839 (request_gc: 'The caller is THE running thread'; gc_requested plain bool; barrier at 802-822)
assumptions.what: At most one thread can hit the alloc trigger (only the lock-holder allocates), so the requester unconditionally becomes the collector and the barrier only ever waits for peers to park.
assumptions.breaks_because: With simultaneous mutators, two threads cross their triggers concurrently: both set gc_requested, both enter the barrier, and each waits forever for the OTHER (which is in_ml, unparked, spinning in the same barrier) — mutual deadlock. Needs collector ELECTION: one CAS-winner collects, losers convert themselves into safepoint parkers.

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-runtime/src/sched.rs:222-233 (SchedInner.running single bool) + the all_parked predicate at sched.rs:803-815
assumptions.what: 'Exactly one mutator' is encoded as a single boolean token; the STW barrier is 'every registered non-me handle has in_ml==false && parked_roots.is_some()'.
assumptions.breaks_because: Breaking the giant lock removes the single token: 'running' must become a count of unparked mutators (upstream threads_in_heap), and the barrier waits for count→1 (just the collector). Every acquire/release/yield/safepoint helper in sched.rs (424-766) encodes the single-token protocol and must be rewritten around the count.

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-runtime/src/interpreter/mod.rs:4631-4663 (65536-step safepoint poll) + sched.rs:881-883 (gc_requested_relaxed actually locks the scheduler Mutex)
assumptions.what: Polling the GC-request flag may take the full scheduler mutex because only one thread runs at a time (zero contention by construction).
assumptions.breaks_because: N simultaneous mutators each locking the scheduler mutex every 65536 steps serializes the hot loops on that mutex — the giant lock reappears through the back door. Must become a genuine AtomicBool (the 'relaxed' name is currently a misnomer). The poll cadence also now bounds time-to-safepoint × slowest thread = GC pause latency.

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-runtime/src/interpreter/mod.rs:4702-4719 (top-of-step trigger reads shared used_words) + mod.rs:554-561/1608 (gc_trigger_words per-thread mirror of a plain pub usize, sched.rs:313)
assumptions.what: The GC trigger check is an unsynchronized read of the ONE space's used counter against a per-thread cached threshold set once at build time.
assumptions.breaks_because: With per-thread nurseries the check must become 'MY nursery is full' (safe, single-owner) feeding a GLOBAL policy (Σ nursery used vs budget) — the current shared-read races peer allocations, and the plain-usize Runtime field races any future dynamic resizing. Upstream's shape: nursery-full → try to acquire a fresh segment; GC only when the allocation-space budget is exhausted.

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-runtime/src/interpreter/mod.rs:2902-2928 (parked_guards held across the whole collection) + mod.rs:3284-3318 (audit_cross_stack_residual re-locks the same parked_roots)
assumptions.what: The collector may hold every peer's parked_roots MutexGuard for the full collection, and may later re-lock the same mutexes, because the sequencing (guards dropped at 3108 before the audit) is single-collector-linear.
assumptions.breaks_because: Any redesign that overlaps these phases, adds a second collector, or lets an exiting thread's exit_parked (sched.rs:634-653) contend differently creates a lock-ordering hazard; the discipline 'guards dropped before any re-lock' is currently implicit in statement order, not structure.

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-runtime/src/sched.rs:174-191 (rts_park_root: ONE Mutex<Option<SendPtrMut>> cell per thread) + mod.rs:2916-2925 (collector forwards it)
assumptions.what: A blocked RTS call carries at most ONE RTS-local heap-pointer cell needing forwarding across a park.
assumptions.breaks_because: The roadmap parks more paths (recv/send/stdin/OS.Process.system). Any RTS function holding TWO live heap refs across a park (e.g. src+dst arrays) silently loses the second — the collector moves the object and the post-park re-derive reads freed from-space. Needs a small Vec of cells (and the discipline documented at rts.rs:248-266 extended).

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-runtime/src/interpreter/mod.rs:4021-4024 (region_gc_used_ptr = addr_of!(s.used) baked into JIT region ExnCtx) + gc.rs:413-417 (replace_storage mutates in place, MemorySpace struct itself never moves)
assumptions.what: The address of the heap's `used` counter is stable for the process lifetime because the one MemorySpace lives permanently inside the Runtime's HeapCell.
assumptions.breaks_because: Per-thread nurseries in a growable registry (Vec/HashMap) relocate MemorySpace structs on growth, dangling the raw counter pointer baked into compiled regions' inline GC polls. Nurseries need stable addresses (Box each / arena) or the JIT poll needs an indirection.

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-runtime/src/export.rs:50-155 (snapshot/snapshot_gated: by_addr HashMap keyed on raw addresses, drain derefs headers) + rts.rs:1911-1943 (poly_export reachable from running bytecode)
assumptions.what: The heap is frozen during the export graph walk: no object moves (address-keyed interning) and no field is concurrently mutated, guaranteed today because the exporting thread holds the giant lock and all peers are parked/blocked.
assumptions.breaks_because: With simultaneous mutators, a peer can mutate a ref mid-walk (torn snapshot, or a pointer overwritten between the ptr_ok check and the deref → wild read even in trusted mode) and a peer-triggered GC mid-walk moves objects out from under by_addr. PolyExport must become a stop-the-world root request (upstream runs the exporter as a root-request with the world stopped) — same for any future SaveState/bic writer that walks live heap.

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-runtime/src/rts.rs:343-353 (RtsSafeSpaces.ranges: '≤4 ranges — the image spaces plus THE live alloc space') + mod.rs:1761 (rts_safe_spaces)
assumptions.what: Untrusted-mode space membership includes exactly one runtime alloc range.
assumptions.breaks_because: Valid objects living in a peer thread's nursery would fail contains_with_header/validate_obj_fit and get rejected (clean halt / placeholder) — a correctness break for --untrusted multi-threaded runs. The bundle must carry all nursery ranges and be re-derived after every GC (it already must be re-derived post-park; see the stale-snapshot warnings at rts.rs:2634 and 6279).

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-runtime/src/interpreter/mod.rs:893-906 (thread_obj_addr mirror: 'only ever DEREFERENCED by the running mutator under the giant lock, mutually exclusive with any collection')
assumptions.what: Reading a peer's ML-thread-object address mirror can never observe a mid-GC (stale) value because reader (the one mutator) and mover (the collector) are mutually exclusive via the giant lock.
assumptions.breaks_because: With N mutators, PolyThreadBroadcastInterrupt/MakeRequest running on thread A while the collector moves thread B's object is impossible under STW (A is parked) — but A reading the mirror while B's OWNER concurrently materializes/replaces the object is a new mutator-vs-mutator race the giant lock used to exclude. Needs the STW fence plus a rule for mutator-to-mutator object access (upstream guards these with schedLock).

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-runtime/src/gc.rs:121-125 + mod.rs:873-879 (forward_stack_slot: no tagged-bit filter on stack slots)
assumptions.what: A raw integer on a stack that happens to fall inside from-space is acceptable collateral (it gets rewritten to a to-space address) because from-space is one bounded range.
assumptions.breaks_because: With N nurseries the union of from-space ranges is much larger, so the probability that an untagged scratch integer (or a raw PC into image code) aliases SOME from-space range grows with thread count × nursery size; each false hit silently corrupts a stack slot. Inherited conservatism that scales badly — worth measuring, and worth keeping nurseries in a compact reserved VA region to bound it.

assumptions.what: A quiesced (between-run_until) thread's identity roots are forwarded in the published box and written back only at its next acquire, because the collector must never write into a struct whose owner runs lock-free driver code.
assumptions.breaks_because: Every NEW acquire/exit path the multi-mutator redesign adds must reproduce this exact retract-then-copy-back dance (free_published_box); a path that retracts the slot without applying the deferred write-back resurrects a from-space thread_object pointer — the bug class is invisible until a GC happens to move the identity object during a quiescent window.

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-runtime/src/space.rs:131-153 (try_alloc doc: 'Exhaustion is TERMINAL BY DESIGN — callers must NOT try GC-then-retry' — the alloc-pointer-caching hazard) + the ~30 alloc_or_exit sites in rts.rs
assumptions.what: A GC can NEVER fire inside an RTS helper or between the try_alloc and the writes that initialize the object, so raw body pointers may be cached across sequential allocations within one RTS call.
assumptions.breaks_because: A naive nursery-full → collect-and-retry inside allocation would move objects while dozens of RTS helpers (rts.rs:2281, 3435, 5722-5826 etc.) hold raw pointers from an earlier alloc in the same call. The invariant to preserve: nursery exhaustion mid-call must be satisfied by acquiring a FRESH segment (never by moving objects); GC happens only at step boundaries / explicit safepoints — exactly upstream's FindAllocationSpace shape (processes.cpp:947).

required_changes: Generalize Collector (gc.rs) from one from-space to a set: from_spaces as sorted disjoint (start,end) ranges; contains_polyword/contains_raw_ptr/forward_impl do a range-set lookup; from_objects becomes per-space maps (or one merged sorted map — it already binary-searches, so merging N sorted per-nursery walks is cheap).

Change collect()'s signature from `&mut MemorySpace` to a nursery-set + destination policy. Either (a) one to-space sized ≥ Σ nursery capacities (simplest, keeps the bump_to overflow assert honest), or (b) upstream-style promotion into shared local space(s). If multiple destinations, cheney_scan needs per-destination scan pointers looping to a joint fixpoint.

Replace HeapCell/heap_mut single-heap with: a per-thread nursery owned outright by its mutator (no lock; allocation stays a plain bump) + a Runtime-side nursery registry mutated only at nursery create/replace and read by the collector under STW. Nursery structs need stable addresses (Box them) so region_gc_used_ptr's baked pointer and RtsContext.alloc_space borrows stay valid.

Collector election in sched.rs request_gc: CAS a collector-owner token (gc_requested becomes an AtomicBool or owner id); losers detect the lost race and safepoint_park instead of barrier-waiting; replace the single `running` bool with an unparked-mutator count and make the barrier wait for count==1.
gc_requested_relaxed must become a real atomic load (today it takes the scheduler mutex per poll — N hot loops would serialize on it).

Interpreter::gc(): build the registry the same way (the ThreadRoots-registry design already anticipates N entries), but (a) take from-space ranges from ALL nurseries, (b) read image roots from Runtime.image_roots under its lock instead of self.image_mutable_roots.clone(), (c) collect every nursery every cycle (no subset collection — there are no remembered sets for nursery-to-nursery pointers, and stores are unrestricted).

Per-thread trigger: nursery-full at a step boundary → acquire fresh segment from a global budget; budget exhausted → request_gc. Kill the shared used>=trigger read (mod.rs:4702) in favor of a per-nursery check; make the global accounting atomic.

Extend the audit: in_old over the range set; walk all destination spaces and all nurseries post-collection; keep audit_cross_stack_residual and the gc_audit_residual teeth (the negative-control pattern already exists — reuse it for every new invariant).
Wrap PolyExport/PolyExportPortable (and any future heap-walking RTS) in the STW handshake — run the snapshot as the collector-equivalent with all mutators parked.
RtsSafeSpaces: include all nursery ranges and re-derive after any park/GC; rts_park_root: generalize the single cell to a small Vec of cells before parking more IO paths.

scrub_below_sp / apply_fixups / ForkRoots stay valid under STW but re-verify every new acquire path reproduces the quiesced deferred-identity-write-back (free_published_box) and the publish-before-decrement ordering (B1/B2) — these invariants are positional, not type-enforced, in the new sched code.

Keep forwarding single-collector (plain tombstones). If collection is ever parallelized, forward_object's header check-then-install must become a CAS on the length word (upstream quick_gc.cpp:131-155) and bump_to must become per-worker allocation regions.
hazards: Concurrent-trigger deadlock: two mutators crossing their triggers both set gc_requested and both spin in the barrier waiting for the other to park — neither is parked, so the STW barrier livelocks forever. The single-threaded test suite can never catch this; only an N-thread alloc-storm test will.

To-space overflow assert: bump_to's release assert is sized to ONE from-space. Evacuating N nurseries can legitimately exceed it — the panic fires mid-collection with the heap half-tombstoned (unrecoverable, worse than OOM). A naive port that just enlarges the scratch vec silently doubles peak RSS × N.

GC-retry-on-nursery-full is the trap the codebase already documents (space.rs:131-142): ~30 RTS helpers cache raw body pointers across sequential allocations in one call. Nursery exhaustion mid-RTS-call must be satisfied by a fresh segment (objects never move), never by collecting — upstream's FindAllocationSpace shape exists precisely for this.

Cross-nursery pointers are UNRESTRICTED and arise immediately: fork hands the child a closure allocated in the parent's nursery; a Mutex-protected record in nursery A is mutated by thread B to point into nursery B; closure word-0 raw code pointers and mid-object PC values on A's stack can point into B's nursery code objects. There are no write barriers/remembered sets, so EVERY collection must treat ALL nurseries as from-space — partial/per-thread collection is unsound without new machinery.

The collector scans its per-Interpreter clone of image_mutable_roots, not the shared Runtime list — a root region registered by one thread after a peer's Interpreter was built is invisible to that peer-as-collector (missed roots → UAF). Currently masked because registration happens only at single-threaded load time.

forward_stack_slot's deliberate lack of a tag filter means any raw integer aliasing ANY from-space range gets rewritten; with N nurseries the alias probability scales with total nursery VA. A rare, non-deterministic stack-corruption class that only statistical soak testing surfaces.

region_gc_used_ptr bakes addr_of!(space.used) into compiled JIT regions; moving nurseries into a growable Vec relocates the MemorySpace struct and turns every compiled region's inline GC poll into a wild read — crashes only under --jit/--whole-region with threads, long after the refactor.
gc_requested_relaxed is named 'relaxed' but takes the scheduler mutex — a naive port keeps it and silently re-serializes all mutators on the poll cadence, making 'parallelism' benchmark at 1×.

Export/snapshot in trusted mode chases raw pointers with zero validation; without a STW fence a concurrent mutator makes this UB (not just a torn image) — the check-then-deref in value_for/intern is unsynchronized.

The quiesced-capture protocol (collector must NOT write a parked-between-run_until thread's Interpreter fields; identity roots deferred to the owner's next acquire) is enforced by statement order in exactly two functions — any new acquire path that retracts parked_roots without calling free_published_box resurrects from-space thread_object pointers, visible only when a GC happens during a quiescent window.

Guard-ordering: gc() holds all peers' parked_roots guards for the whole collection, then drops them, then the audit re-locks them. Any refactor that interleaves these phases (or runs the audit inside collect) self-deadlocks on the second lock of the same mutex.

ForkRoots (mod.rs:1122-1175): the parent-published two-slot root-set for a not-yet-started child is easy to forget when rewriting registration — the B1 TOCTOU (registered, not in_ml, roots=None → skipped stack → UAF) is the exact bug class the current code structurally eliminated; the counted-barrier rewrite must preserve register-with-published-roots atomicity.
maps.design_opinion: Keep the collector single-threaded and strictly stop-the-world; break only the MUTATOR lock. The gc.rs core is well-factored for this: forwarding is already membership-driven (contains + find_object) and the root walk already iterates a ThreadRoots registry built for N entries, so generalizing from-space to a sorted range-set over per-thread nurseries is a localized change — the genuinely hard, bug-prone work is in sched.rs (collector election via a CAS'd owner token, replacing the `running` bool with an unparked-mutator count, and keeping the publish-before-unpark invariants B1/B2 structural through the rewrite). For evacuation, prefer upstream's shape — collect ALL nurseries every cycle into a shared destination (promotion), since unrestricted cross-nursery stores make partial collection unsound without write barriers, and a Σ-capacity to-space doubles peak memory. Fix the three shared-read footguns first (gc_requested must be a real atomic; image roots read from Runtime, not the per-thread clone; per-nursery trigger accounting), and wrap PolyExport in the STW handshake. Upstream's model, for reference: gMem holds a global table of local spaces flagged as allocation areas; each TaskData carves private nursery segments via FindAllocationSpace (downward bump through per-thread allocPointer/allocLimit, no synchronization inside a segment, allocSize doubling); a thread that cannot get a segment raises a root request that pauses all ML threads; the minor GC (quick_gc.cpp) then evacuates every allocation space into mutable/immutable local spaces with roots = permanent mutable segments + each thread's stack, parallelized over a GC task farm whose workers claim spaces by ownership and install forwarding tombstones with a CAS on the length word; the major GC is a parallel bitmap-mark/compact/update over all local spaces, after which allocation areas are emptied and every thread's alloc pointers are zeroed to re-request segments. Finally: extend the existing gc_audit_residual negative-control pattern to every new invariant BEFORE the rewrite — this codebase's history (the fairness test, the H1/H2 controls) shows the test instrument is what finds the scheduler bugs, and a multi-mutator GC bug without an audit is a nondeterministic UAF weeks later.

maps.area: Scheduler and the giant lock itself (crates/polyml-runtime/src/sched.rs; safepoint/park/thread-RTS machinery in crates/polyml-runtime/src/interpreter/mod.rs; RtsPark in crates/polyml-runtime/src/rts.rs)

assumptions.what: A single process-wide boolean IS the mutual exclusion: 'true while exactly one thread is running bytecode (and may touch the heap)'. Every helper's wait loop is `while inner.running || inner.gc_requested`.
assumptions.breaks_because: With 2+ simultaneous mutators there is no single 'the running thread'. Everything downstream that says 'we hold the giant lock so X is race-free' (heap access, mutex words, thread-object words, the ForkRoots publish window) loses its justification. `running` must be replaced by per-thread `in_ml` as the authoritative state plus a count/barrier for GC only.

assumptions.what: 'the giant lock IS the heap's mutual exclusion' — heap_mut hands out `&mut Option<MemorySpace>` to any caller claiming in_ml==true.
assumptions.breaks_because: Two mutators both in_ml would each obtain `&mut MemorySpace` to the SAME space — instant aliasing UB. space.rs:143-153 try_alloc is a non-atomic bump (`self.used = new_used`): two racing allocations return overlapping objects even before UB formality. Requires per-thread nurseries (upstream TaskData::allocPointer/allocLimit, processes.cpp FindAllocationSpace ~:945) carved from a locked global pool.

assumptions.what: Acquire = publish roots, wait `while running || gc_requested`, retract roots, set running=true + in_ml=true, notify_all on mutator_wake (the fairness fix for pure-compute takers).
assumptions.breaks_because: The whole publish-while-queued discipline exists because waiting-to-run is a parked state. Under parallelism there is no queue to run — 'acquire' collapses to 'wait out any in-flight GC, set own in_ml, retract roots'. The take-announcement notify_all (lines 443-450) exists solely to unblock yield hand-offs and becomes dead.

assumptions.what: First-acquire registers+publishes+waits under ONE inner-lock acquisition (H1 structural fix); crucially it WAITS OUT `gc_requested` BEFORE pushing into the registry so a mid-collection registration cannot appear after the collector's snapshot.
assumptions.breaks_because: This wait-before-register discipline is the part that MUST survive: under parallelism registrations can genuinely race a collection. But its sibling register_thread_published (sched.rs:388-403, the fork path) has NO gc_requested wait — sound today only because the forking parent IS the sole running mutator so no collection can be in flight. With 2+ mutators a peer's collection can be mid-snapshot while a parent registers a child → child's ForkRoots unforwarded this cycle → stale/from-space closure → UAF.

assumptions.what: The forked child's first acquire keeps the parent-published ForkRoots as the slot until it wins the lock (B1). Assumes the child transitions directly from 'parked with ForkRoots' to 'the one running mutator'.
assumptions.breaks_because: The mechanism generalizes (keep-published until GC-clear + own in_ml set), but the retract-then-run step must still be ordered against a collector: retraction may only happen when no collection is in flight. The current code gets that ordering from the `running || gc_requested` wait; the port must preserve 'retract only under gc_requested==false' even though `running` disappears.

assumptions.what: Leaving run_until publishes roots then clears `running` (B2: no release-while-registered with parked_roots==None); the terminal exit quiesces first so the collector's write-back cannot race lock-free driver code.
assumptions.breaks_because: Publish-before-clearing-own-in_ml stays load-bearing (the collector's barrier tests ¬in_ml ∧ Some). But clearing the shared `running` from here becomes clearing only one's OWN in_ml. The comment at mod.rs:4601-4605 ('a RESUMABLE stash only occurs on the single-threaded CLI loop, where no peer can fire a GC') is single-mutator reasoning baked into the soundness argument of the fresh-capture re-acquire path and must be re-proven.

assumptions.what: H2: `running` is cleared IFF the exiting thread actually holds it; two distinct exit paths chosen by whether we hold the lock (Drop discriminates on own in_ml).
assumptions.breaks_because: In a multi-mutator world there is no shared `running` to clobber, so H2 restates as: 'each thread clears its OWN in_ml exactly once per acquire and never touches a peer's state; deregistration + parked_roots=None happen under one lock, after waiting out any in-flight collection.' The two exit paths merge into one. But note exit_parked's wait-out-gc loop (638-644) is called by the PARENT on the child handle in the fork spawn-failure path (mod.rs:2678) while the parent is still in_ml — under parallelism a peer's collector would be waiting for the parent to park while the parent waits for gc_requested to clear: deadlock. Any in_ml thread that waits for gc_requested without parking itself first deadlocks the barrier.

assumptions.what: The blocking-wait bracket (mutex-block, condvar-wait, RtsPark): publish → clear running → block on block_wake → wait `running||gc_requested` → retract → running=true.
assumptions.breaks_because: This is the pattern that SURVIVES (it is exactly upstream ThreadReleaseMLMemory/ThreadUseMLMemory around WaitInfinite), but reshaped: it must stop toggling the shared `running` and instead toggle only own in_ml, and the re-acquire must still gate on gc_requested so a woken waiter never mutates its stack mid-collection. The collector-notify at every publish (line 672 etc.) must be kept — the barrier's liveness depends on every park path nudging collector_wake.

assumptions.what: GC safepoint: poll `gc_requested_relaxed()` per 64K steps; if set, publish + clear running + wait for collection + re-acquire. This is the ONLY mechanism by which an executing thread becomes stoppable.
assumptions.breaks_because: This generalizes into THE stop-the-world mechanism (upstream's threadRequest + inMLHeap + allStopped spin, processes.cpp:1369-1399) — but two things break: (a) gc_requested_relaxed (sched.rs:881-883) is not relaxed at all, it takes the scheduler Mutex — N parallel mutators hammering one mutex every 64K steps is a contention point and must become a real atomic (or upstream's poisoned stack-limit / per-thread trap flag, InterruptCode); (b) the collector currently keeps `running==true` while it spins in the barrier so no peer can start running — with `running` gone, exclusivity must come solely from 'gc_requested blocks every acquire/reacquire path', which the wait loops already check, so keep that property total.

assumptions.what: 'The caller is THE running thread (only the running thread allocates, so only it can hit the trigger).' gc_requested is set unconditionally (no ownership/election); the barrier waits for every OTHER registered handle to satisfy ¬in_ml ∧ parked_roots.is_some(); collect() runs with inner RELEASED, exclusivity argued from `gc_requested && running && barrier`.
assumptions.breaks_because: With N mutators each owning a nursery, two threads can exhaust simultaneously and BOTH call request_gc: both set gc_requested=true, then each spins in the barrier waiting for the other (each is in_ml) → permanent deadlock; or with unlucky interleaving both pass and run collect() concurrently → double Cheney → heap destruction. Needs collector ELECTION (CAS a requester token / upstream's `threadRequest` slot + 'wait for any other requests' loop, processes.cpp:912-941) with losers parking at the safepoint like everyone else and re-checking the trigger after the winner's GC. The conjunctive barrier predicate itself (¬in_ml AND Some) generalizes verbatim and must NOT be weakened.

assumptions.what: The entire yield machinery — waiters count, the waiters==0 early-return, the forced hand-off spin ('wait until a peer has actually TAKEN the lock'), the notify-at-every-take — exists only because exactly-one-mutator means compute-bound peers starve without explicit hand-offs.
assumptions.breaks_because: Under real parallelism there is nothing to hand off — the OS scheduler runs all mutators. yield_ml_memory, `waiters`, the hand-off spin, and both historical fairness fixes become dead code. What must REMAIN at the poll site is: the gc_requested park, check_thread_requests (interrupt/kill), and take_interrupt. Deleting the yield without keeping those three re-opens GC/interrupt latency holes.

assumptions.what: H1: 'every registered thread that is not the running thread has parked_roots == Some'. parked_roots is the collector's ONLY route to a peer's stack; None is structurally unreachable.
assumptions.breaks_because: Restated for multi-mutator: 'registered ∧ ¬in_ml ⟹ parked_roots is Some' — the predicate already exists in per-thread form in the barrier (sched.rs:814), so H1 survives with `running` simply deleted from its statement. The structural enforcement (no bare register_thread; register paths publish-then-push under one lock; exits clear slot+registry under one lock) must be preserved exactly; the port's new risk is the register_thread_published gc-window (see above) and any NEW park path that clears in_ml before publishing.

assumptions.what: The collector snapshots the registry, holds every peer's parked_roots MutexGuard across forward+fixup, captures its OWN roots directly, and writes forwarded values back into peers' Interpreter fields via raw pointers — all justified by 'every other thread is parked and blocked until gc_requested clears'.
assumptions.breaks_because: This is stop-the-world and generalizes as-is, BUT the happens-before edge 'parked threads resume only after fixup' currently comes from their wait loops re-checking gc_requested under `inner` after the collector clears it (sched.rs:834-836). Any port that lets a woken block_until_event waiter touch its stack before reacquire (which gates on gc_requested) re-opens a write-back race. Also the guard-held-across-collection pattern means an exiting thread's exit_parked serializes behind the collection — keep that.

assumptions.what: The SML Thread.Mutex fast path (Thread.sml:534-561: tryLockMutex → lockMutex → PolyThreadMutexBlock retry loop; unlock: atomicReset → PolyThreadMutexUnlock) is implemented as PLAIN non-atomic read-modify-write on the mutex object's word 0, serialized only by the giant lock.
assumptions.breaks_because: Upstream runs these interlocked: bytecode.cpp:1496-1542 and 803-823 wrap lockMutex/tryLockMutex/atomicReset/atomicIncr/atomicDecr in a global `PLocker pl(&mutexLock)`, and InterpreterReleaseMutex (bytecode.cpp:2465-2471) likewise. Two parallel mutators doing lockMutex simultaneously with our plain RMW can BOTH observe old==TAGGED(0) and both 'acquire' — mutual exclusion silently broken (and a Rust data race = UB). Must become atomic RMW on the heap word (fetch_add(2) for lock, CAS 0→1 for trylock, swap(0) for atomicReset) or a faithful global mutexLock.

assumptions.what: The runtime side of a contended unlock re-writes the mutex word to TAGGED(0) — an extra defensive write upstream does NOT do (processes.cpp:443-460: 'The caller has already set the variable to 1 (unlocked)'; MutexUnlock only signals waiters).
assumptions.breaks_because: Under parallelism this extra plain write can clobber a concurrent lock acquisition: peer CAS-locks the word (0→1) between our unlock's atomicReset and the RTS reset → we overwrite 1 with 0 → two threads inside the SML critical section. The reset must be deleted (upstream-faithful) or folded into the single atomic swap in atomicReset. Same for the CondVarWait/WaitUntil preamble resets (mod.rs:2267, 2313), which port upstream's AtomicallyReleaseMutex and must become an atomic swap.

assumptions.what: Lost-wakeup prevention is a GLOBAL generation counter: sample block_gen BEFORE releasing the giant lock, wait for it to advance; every unlock/wake/exit/interrupt bumps it and wakes ALL waiters. Upstream instead re-checks the mutex VALUE under schedLock (processes.cpp:399-405) and signals per-thread threadLock only for waiters whose blockMutex matches (443-460).
assumptions.breaks_because: The generation scheme stays CORRECT under parallelism (block_gen has its own mutex; sample-before-release closes the window) but every mutex/condvar event wakes every blocked thread process-wide (thundering herd), and correctness now also depends on ordering: the mutex-word write must precede notify_block_event in the release order, or a waiter can sample, see a stale locked word, and wait for a bump that already happened. A naive port that reorders the atomic word-write after the notify reopens the lost-wake.

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-runtime/src/interpreter/mod.rs:1959-1977 (thread_obj_write contract), 2049-2061 (make_thread_request writing TARGET requestCopy word 3), 2002-2016 (set_own_thread_flags / clear_own_request_copy), 2424-2434 (BroadcastInterrupt reading PEER flags via thread_obj_addr mirror), 2355-2373 (CondVarWake reading target flags)
assumptions.what: 'The caller must be the running mutator (giant lock held) — thread-object words are ML-mutable shared heap state, and the lock is their mutual exclusion.' Runtime-side heap-word mutations: mutex word 0 (Unlock/CondVarWait/WaitUntil); thread-object word 0 (id at fork, mod.rs:2603), word 1 flags (AsynchOnce→Synch downgrade), word 3 requestCopy (cross-thread write in make_thread_request!), word 4 mlStackSize (mod.rs:2470).
assumptions.breaks_because: Word 1 is ALSO written directly by SML bytecode (setAttributes via RunCall.storeWord) and word 3 is written by the runtime INTO A PEER while — under parallelism — that peer is running and reading/writing the same words. Every cross-thread thread-object word access becomes a mutator-vs-mutator data race (UB in Rust, not merely a stale read). All must go through atomic loads/stores; BroadcastInterrupt's 'peers parked, no GC in flight, so race-free' justification for dereferencing the thread_obj_addr mirror is void (the heap-deref stays safe — STW GC means no motion while any mutator runs — but the word reads race).

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-runtime/src/rts.rs:187-341 (RtsPark, park_while, park_while_with_root, CURRENT_PARK) + interpreter/mod.rs:7684-7694 (arming) + sched.rs:191 (rts_park_root)
assumptions.what: Blocking syscalls release the giant lock across the kernel wait with roots published; `f` is NATIVE-data-only by contract; at most ONE extra heap-root cell (rts_park_root) is forwardable per park; arming gated on registry_len()>1.
assumptions.breaks_because: The mechanism survives verbatim (it is the per-thread publish/park pattern), but its criticality INVERTS: today a non-parked blocking RTS call merely freezes peers; under parallelism it blocks the stop-the-world barrier forever — every GC waits on one thread stuck in recv/stdin-read/OS.Process.system. The still-unparked set (recv/send/stdin/system — unparked because their buffers are live heap pointers) becomes a GC-liveness bug, and the single rts_park_root cell is insufficient for calls carrying more than one heap ref. GC-forwarding of RTS-local heap refs across parks becomes mandatory, not an enhancement.

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-runtime/src/interpreter/mod.rs:4702-4708 (per-step trigger check) + 7702-7712 (RtsContext holds &mut MemorySpace across an entire RTS call) + 6823-6834 (allocate)
assumptions.what: GC trigger = the running thread compares the ONE shared space's used_words against gc_trigger_words each step; every allocation (opcodes, fork's thread object, RTS ctx) funnels through the single space; sched.rs:770 codifies 'only the running thread allocates, so only it can hit the trigger'.
assumptions.breaks_because: Under parallelism the used_words read is racy, the &mut aliasing is UB, and the trigger concept itself dissolves: with per-thread nurseries the trigger becomes 'my nursery is exhausted → grab a segment from the global pool (locked) → if the pool is exhausted, request GC' (upstream FindAllocationSpace). RtsContext must carry the calling thread's nursery, not the global space.

assumptions.what: SIGINT is one process-global AtomicBool consumed by whichever thread polls first (today: the one running mutator, i.e. deterministic); PolyFinish is a process-global consumed-on-read flag checked at every step.
assumptions.breaks_because: With N pollers, Ctrl-C is delivered to a RANDOM thread (upstream routes it via broadcastInterrupt / the console thread), and PolyFinish's clear-on-read means ONE thread halts while the others keep running — 'exit' stops being an exit. Both need explicit multi-thread semantics (route interrupt to the root/console thread or broadcast; PolyFinish = KILL-broadcast + process exit).

assumptions.what: Several arms choose stub behavior vs real blocking by sampling registry_len() at one instant, assuming thread count cannot change while this thread runs (only the running mutator can fork).
assumptions.breaks_because: Under parallelism a peer can fork/exit between the check and the block. The blocking side stays safe (exits bump block_gen so a stranded waiter wakes), but the fallback side can misfire: a thread could take the single-threaded no-op stub path while a second thread is being created, changing SML-visible mutex semantics for that call. The gates need to become monotonic ('real threads mode' once any fork has happened) rather than instantaneous counts.

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-runtime/src/sched.rs:53-71, 108-111 (SendPtr/SendPtrMut/SendRoots unsafe Send: 'dereferenced only by the lock-holding collector')
assumptions.what: All unsafe Send/Sync justifications are phrased in terms of the giant lock / the single lock-holder.
assumptions.breaks_because: The pointers remain sound under STW GC (sole collector, all mutators parked), but every SAFETY comment's premise ('the giant lock') disappears; a port that keeps the impls without re-deriving the argument from the new barrier invariant will silently rot. HeapCell's Sync in particular becomes FALSE as written once two mutators hold in_ml — it must be re-justified per-nursery.

required_changes: Replace SchedInner::running with per-thread in_ml as the authoritative execution state; delete waiters + yield_ml_memory + the forced-hand-off/notify-at-take machinery (interpreter cooperative_yield becomes a no-op or is removed); acquire/release helpers reshape to: acquire = wait-out gc_requested (parked, roots published) → set own in_ml → retract roots; release = publish roots → clear own in_ml → notify collector_wake.

Turn the GC request into an ELECTED token: CAS/queue a collector identity (upstream threadRequest, processes.cpp:912-941). Losing requesters must PARK at the safepoint (publish + clear in_ml) and re-check the trigger after the winner finishes — never spin in the barrier while in_ml (deadlock).

Keep the stop-the-world barrier predicate exactly conjunctive — for every OTHER registered handle: ¬in_ml ∧ parked_roots.is_some() (sched.rs:803-815) — and make it the ONLY stop mechanism. Add a way to hasten in_ml peers to their safepoint (per-thread AtomicBool trap flag or upstream's poisoned stack-limit InterruptCode; the 65536-step poll is the floor).

Convert gc_requested_relaxed from a mutex acquisition (sched.rs:881-883) into a genuine atomic (or fold into the per-thread trap flag); keep the acquire-side authoritative re-check under the scheduler lock.

Add the gc_requested wait (or handshake join) to register_thread_published (sched.rs:388-403) — the fork registration path currently relies on 'the parent is the sole running mutator' to avoid racing a collector's registry snapshot.

Merge exit_running/exit_parked into one exit path: deregister + clear own in_ml + parked_roots=None under one lock, after waiting out any in-flight collection — and audit every call site so no in_ml thread ever waits for gc_requested without first parking (the fork spawn-failure path mod.rs:2665-2690 does today).

Make every mutex-protocol heap-word operation interlocked: EXTINSTR_LOCK_MUTEX = atomic fetch_add(2) with old==TAGGED(0) test; TRY_LOCK_MUTEX = CAS TAGGED(0)→TAGGED(1); ATOMIC_RESET = atomic swap(TAGGED(0)) with old==TAGGED(1) test; ATOMIC_INCR/DECR/EXCH_ADD = atomic fetch_add/sub — mirroring upstream bytecode.cpp:1496-1542/803-823's mutexLock; simplest faithful port is one global mutexLock Mutex shared by all these opcode handlers plus the RTS-side resets, optimizable to per-word AtomicUsize later.

Remove (or make atomic-swap and upstream-faithful) the runtime-side reset_mutex_word in the PolyThreadMutexUnlock arm (mod.rs:2241-2247) — upstream's MutexUnlock only signals waiters; the extra plain write can clobber a concurrent acquisition. The CondVarWait/WaitUntil mutex releases (mod.rs:2267/2313) become atomic swaps (AtomicallyReleaseMutex).

Make all thread-object word accesses atomic (words 0,1,3,4: id, flags, requestCopy, mlStackSize) on BOTH the runtime side (thread_obj_read/write, make_thread_request's cross-thread requestCopy store, BroadcastInterrupt/CondVarWake flag reads) and — eventually — the SML STORE_WORD path that setAttributes uses; decide and document the PolyWord shared-word memory model (relaxed AtomicUsize views) since plain *mut PolyWord racing is UB in Rust regardless of x86 behavior.

Introduce per-thread allocation nurseries: Runtime stops exposing heap_mut()'s &mut MemorySpace to mutators; each ThreadContext owns an allocPointer/allocLimit segment refilled from a locked global pool; RtsContext carries the caller's nursery; the GC trigger becomes pool exhaustion. The GC handshake must collect every thread's nursery bounds (extend SendRoots or the handle) so the collector can scan/forward all of them.

Keep RtsPark/park_while_blocking as-is in shape but extend coverage: parking becomes mandatory for ALL blocking RTS calls (recv/send/stdin/OS.Process.system) because an unparked blocker now stalls every GC, which requires generalizing rts_park_root from one cell to a small root list and GC-forwarding RTS-local heap refs across parks.

Define multi-thread semantics for the process-global signals: SIGINT routed to a designated (root/console) thread or broadcast per upstream BroadcastInterrupt; PolyFinish becomes KILL-broadcast + orderly stop of all mutators instead of a consumed-on-read per-thread flag.

Re-derive every unsafe Send/Sync justification (SendPtr, SendPtrMut, SendRoots, HeapCell) and every 'giant lock held ⇒ race-free' SAFETY comment against the new invariant set; restate H1 as 'registered ∧ ¬in_ml ⟹ parked_roots is Some' and H2 as 'a thread clears only its OWN in_ml, exactly once per acquire', and update the negative-control tests (concurrency_gc_handshake.rs) to fence the restated invariants.
hazards: Dual-requester GC deadlock: with today's request_gc, two simultaneous trigger-crossers both set gc_requested and both spin in the barrier waiting for the OTHER to park (each is in_ml) — a permanent hang, or with unlucky interleaving a double collection. The single-threaded code gives no hint of this because 'only the running thread allocates'.

Weakening the conjunctive barrier predicate: ¬in_ml alone is NOT parked (the fork-TOCTOU comment at sched.rs:810-814). A port that tests only in_ml (or only parked_roots) reintroduces the B1/B4 skipped-stack UAF that the current code closed structurally.

Any in_ml code path that waits for gc_requested to clear without parking first deadlocks the world (existing instance: fork's spawn-failure cleanup calls exit_parked — which waits out gc_requested — while the parent is the running mutator; benign today, fatal under parallelism).

The runtime-side mutex-word resets (PolyThreadMutexUnlock, CondVarWait preamble) are EXTRA plain writes upstream doesn't do on the word; kept as plain stores they can erase a peer's just-taken lock — two threads inside one SML critical section, corrupting user data with no crash.

Lost-wakeup ordering: the block_gen sample-before-release scheme is only correct if the mutex-word update happens-before notify_block_event; making the word atomic while keeping the notify in the wrong order (or sampling block_gen after release) silently reopens missed wakes that today's giant lock masks.

Publish-before-clear ordering: every park path publishes parked_roots BEFORE decrementing its 'in heap' state; inverting the two (easy in a refactor) gives the collector a window where a thread looks stopped but has no reachable roots — GC skips a live stack → UAF at scale.

The parked-thread write-back (ThreadRoots::apply_fixups writes raw pointers INTO a peer's Interpreter) is only sound because woken waiters re-gate on gc_requested before touching their stack; a new wake path (e.g. a timed wait returning early) that skips reacquire's gc_requested check races the collector's fixup.

gc_requested_relaxed is a misnomer — it locks the scheduler mutex; N threads polling it every 64K steps serialize on one cache line, and worse, a naive 'fix' to a真-relaxed atomic without keeping the authoritative re-check under the lock reintroduces the TOCTOU it papers over.

Plain PolyWord heap accesses: beyond mutex/thread-object words, EVERY SML `ref` shared between threads (STORE_WORD/LOAD opcodes) becomes a mutator-vs-mutator data race — formally UB in Rust even when 'benign' on x86. The giant lock is currently the memory model; the port needs an explicit relaxed-atomic story for heap words, or Miri/TSan will (correctly) reject the whole interpreter.

RtsPark's NATIVE-only contract is stress-tested by parallelism: GCs during parks go from rare to routine, so any RTS function that quietly caches a heap pointer across a park (the class the single rts_park_root cell was added for) becomes a frequent UAF; the unparked blocking calls (recv/send/stdin/system) flip from 'freezes peers' to 'blocks every GC forever'.

registry_len()-instant gates (single-threaded stub fallbacks in MutexBlock/CondVarWait, cooperative_yield's fast path, rts_call's park arming) are TOCTOU under parallelism; they must become monotonic mode flags or the same call can nondeterministically take stub vs real semantics.

PolyFinish's consumed-on-read flag: the first thread to step clears it and halts; peers keep executing — 'OS.Process.exit' stops exiting. Similarly SIGINT lands on a random poller instead of the console thread.
maps.design_opinion: Port upstream processes.cpp's model rather than inventing one: per-thread in_ml as the only execution state, a threadRequest-style elected GC request, and the existing parked_roots publish discipline as the root-handoff — that last piece (H1's conjunctive barrier, publish-before-release, keep-published fork handoff, quiesced captures) was explicitly built to generalize and needs almost no change, which is the strongest asset in this codebase. The giant-lock-specific machinery (running, waiters, yield hand-off, the fairness notifies) should be deleted outright, not adapted; the safepoint poll survives as the sole stop-the-world entry, ideally upgraded to a per-thread atomic trap flag so the collector can hasten stragglers like upstream's InterruptCode. For the mutex protocol, start with a faithful single global mutexLock around the interlocked opcodes plus atomic thread-object word accesses (exactly bytecode.cpp), and only then optimize to per-word atomics — correctness first, and it keeps the differential oracle meaningful. The two changes I'd treat as blocking-precondition work before any scheduler surgery: (1) per-thread nurseries with the RtsContext plumbed to them, because every soundness comment currently routes through 'the giant lock IS the heap's mutual exclusion', and (2) an explicit written memory model for shared heap words (relaxed AtomicUsize views over PolyWord), because without it the port is UB by definition no matter how correct the scheduler is. Finally, extend the H1/H2 negative-control tests to the restated invariants before flipping anything on — this project's history shows the concurrency test is the discovery instrument.

maps.area: THE EDGES — untrusted-mode space validation (SafeSpaces/RtsSafeSpaces), export/snapshot, JIT bridge + whole-region boundary, run_until fast path (gc-trigger + safepoint poll), diagnostics/profiling, Session + CLI run path, and every site that enumerates THE single alloc space

assumptions.what: There is exactly ONE dynamic alloc space; SafeSpaces stores only the ≤3 fixed image spaces and the alloc range is 'read live from the interpreter on each check' — as a single Option<SpaceRange>.
assumptions.breaks_because: With N per-thread nurseries, a pointer held by thread A can legitimately point into thread B's nursery (shared heap). A single-range membership test spuriously rejects valid cross-nursery pointers → false InterpError::BadImage halts; the predicate needs the full live-nursery set.

assumptions.what: The live alloc range is derived from THE Runtime heap (alloc_space_ref → runtime.heap_mut()), returning one SpaceRange bounded by capacity_words; reading it unsynchronized is sound because the caller holds the giant lock.
assumptions.breaks_because: Two simultaneous mutators make the unsynchronized UnsafeCell read a data race, and 'capacity end' membership means validating a pointer into a PEER's nursery can read a length word the peer is concurrently writing (torn/uninitialized header read inside the validator).

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-runtime/src/interpreter/mod.rs:1761-1770 (rts_safe_spaces) + mod.rs:7701 (snapshot taken once per RTS call)
assumptions.what: RtsSafeSpaces is a one-shot snapshot (image ranges + ONE alloc range) built BEFORE RTS dispatch and trusted for the whole call.
assumptions.breaks_because: If the RTS call parks (park_while_blocking) or — post-giant-lock — runs concurrently with a peer GC, the snapshotted alloc range points at the FREED from-space Box (collect() does replace_storage). validate_obj_fit (rts.rs:417) then derefs p.sub(1) for any stale pointer that falls in the dead range — a use-after-free READ inside the safety validator itself, plus false rejection of forwarded objects. rts.rs:2632-2636 already documents this staleness for the recv park and works around it per-site by hand.

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-runtime/src/rts.rs:349-427 (RtsSafeSpaces::contains_with_header / space_end_of / validate_obj_fit) and its ~30 consumers (safe_rts_arg_ptr rts.rs:495, safe_rts_arg_obj rts.rs:534, poly_set_code_constant family rts.rs:4501-4771, sockets rts.rs:2219/2243/2492/2514/2582/2682/2757/3167/3194, bigint rts.rs:3735, strings rts.rs:5587, IO rts.rs:5191/5200/6156, reset_mutex rts.rs:6504)
assumptions.what: Every RTS reader validates image-controlled args against the Vec of ranges captured at dispatch time; the ranges are assumed valid for the call's duration.
assumptions.breaks_because: Same staleness as above, multiplied: with N mutators a GC can occur at ANY peer safepoint while this thread is parked, so every one of these consumers can validate against a dead range. The Vec-of-(usize,usize) shape is right for N nurseries but the construction site pushes exactly one alloc range and has no invalidation/generation mechanism.

assumptions.what: A forked child thread hard-codes TRUSTED mode; the comment admits 'a forked child inherits the parent's trust posture is not yet wired'.
assumptions.breaks_because: Under real multi-mutator execution, an untrusted (foreign) image that calls Thread.fork gets its forked computation run with ZERO typed-deref validation — the entire --untrusted guarantee is void the moment 2 mutators exist. Trust posture must live in the shared Runtime, not the per-Interpreter builder.

assumptions.what: The export graph walk raw-derefs every reachable object assuming the heap is FROZEN for the duration (today guaranteed by the calling thread holding the giant lock while all peers are parked or blocked in native-only waits).
assumptions.breaks_because: With simultaneous mutators, peers bump-allocate and initialize objects (set_length_word then body writes, non-atomic) while the walk reads them → torn headers drive build_object/build_code to read garbage lengths; mutable-object writes race the walk. Export must become a stop-the-world operation using the same handshake as GC.

assumptions.what: Untrusted export gates every pointer-follow on the ONE-alloc-range RtsSafeSpaces; an out-of-range pointer silently becomes a Tagged(0) placeholder.
assumptions.breaks_because: With N nurseries, any exported object whose field points into a nursery missing from the range set is SILENTLY replaced by Tagged(0) — a corrupt exported image with no error, the worst failure mode. The header clamp (space_end_of) likewise needs every nursery's end.

assumptions.what: JIT trampoline allocation hands out &mut MemorySpace from the shared UnsafeCell, justified by 'the giant lock is the heap's mutual exclusion' (SAFETY comments at mod.rs:1486-1503, sched.rs:290-296).
assumptions.breaks_because: Two simultaneous mutators JIT-allocating produce two aliasing &mut MemorySpace and race the non-atomic space.rs:143-153 try_alloc bump of `used` — instant UB and two objects at one address. Allocation dispatch must route to the calling thread's own nursery.

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-runtime/src/jit_bridge.rs:522-569 (jit_dispatch_closure_call nested `interp.step()` loop) and crates/polyml-bin/src/main.rs:681-698 (POLY_CHECKPOINT_EVERY loop calling interp.step())
