assumptions.what: Dispatch loops that call step() directly never run run_until's 65536-step safepoint poll — acceptable because single-mutator GC is self-triggered at top-of-step.
assumptions.breaks_because: With multi-mutator STW GC, a thread spinning in either loop never observes gc_requested and never parks → the collector's stop-the-world barrier stalls forever (deadlock). The CLI checkpoint loop additionally never does the acquire_running bracket at all, so under real threads it mutates the heap without holding ML memory.

assumptions.what: One active Interpreter per OS thread, reachable through a thread-local raw pointer; JIT'd native frames may hold heap-pointer bits in Cranelift registers/spills across trampoline calls.
assumptions.breaks_because: The thread-local itself survives N threads, but a nested trampoline (which can now trigger or park for a GC) moves heap objects while heap-pointer bits sit unscanned in the JIT'd caller's native frame — a UAF the single-mutator world only avoided by low GC pressure and the tail-call-only convention (jit_bridge.rs:132-134). More mutators = more GCs = the latent class becomes hot.

assumptions.what: The whole-region back-edge poll reads *gc_used_ptr (raw address of MemorySpace.used inside the Runtime's HeapCell) with a plain native load, against a by-value gc_trigger; the address is 'stable while the heap lives... the poll runs only under the giant lock' (mod.rs:4016-4018).
assumptions.breaks_because: (a) If nurseries live in any movable container (Vec growth on thread registration) the cached pointer dangles; (b) if the counter stays shared, N mutators bump it non-atomically while regions read it plainly — a data race (UB), not just imprecision; (c) a per-nursery trigger recomputed at GC is invisible to the by-value ctx.gc_trigger. Must point at the calling thread's OWN single-writer nursery counter at a stable (Boxed) address.

assumptions.what: A native region only SELF-triggers GC (its poll mirrors the top-of-step threshold check); there is no path for the region to respond to a PEER's GC request.
assumptions.breaks_because: Under multi-mutator STW, a compute-bound region never parks for a peer collector → barrier deadlock. The region poll must also test the shared GC-stop flag (or the runtime must be able to slam the region's per-thread trigger to 0 to force the slow path, which then parks with live_sp published).

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-runtime/src/interpreter/mod.rs:4702-4708 (per-step gc-trigger check) + 556-561/1332/1603-1618 (gc_trigger_words per-interp cache mirrored from runtime)
assumptions.what: Every step reads THE shared heap's used_words through the UnsafeCell and compares against a per-interpreter cached copy of one global trigger.
assumptions.breaks_because: N mutators reading `used` while peers bump it non-atomically is a per-step data race; and one global trigger over one space is meaningless when allocation is distributed. The check must become 'my nursery used vs my nursery trigger' — a thread-local read, which conveniently also makes it cheaper.

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-runtime/src/interpreter/mod.rs:4631-4663 (the 65536-step poll: gc_requested_relaxed / check_thread_requests / take_interrupt / cooperative_yield) + sched.rs:881-883
assumptions.what: The coarse poll assumes gc_requested is cheap to read (it actually LOCKS the sched mutex — 'relaxed' in name only), that cooperative_yield is how peers interleave, and that the process-global SIGINT flag can be consumed by whoever polls first.
assumptions.breaks_because: N threads taking the sched mutex every 65536 steps serializes on it; cooperative_yield is a giant-lock artifact that must vanish; take_interrupt's consume-and-clear delivers Ctrl-C to an arbitrary mutator instead of a defined target (upstream broadcasts / targets the console thread). The poll's new job is the GC-stop handshake and it needs a true atomic with the right acquire/release pairing against roots publication.

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-runtime/src/interpreter/mod.rs:4686-4688 (finish_requested at top of every step) + rts.rs:1853-1903 (FINISH_REQUESTED global, consume-and-clear)
assumptions.what: PolyFinish is a process-global flag; the first step to observe it clears it and halts that interpreter.
assumptions.breaks_because: With N mutators, exactly one thread consumes the finish and returns while peers keep executing — OS.Process.exit no longer terminates the program. It must become a broadcast halt (and its per-step global atomic load becomes a shared-cache-line tax on every core).

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-runtime/src/interpreter/mod.rs:2867-3122 (gc()) + gc.rs:85 (contains_polyword), gc.rs:300-390 (collect over ONE &mut MemorySpace, from_objects for one range), mod.rs:2944+3125-3225 (audit against one from-range)
assumptions.what: The collector takes ONE alloc space, builds a from-object table for one contiguous range, and audits residual pointers against a single [from_lo, from_hi).
assumptions.breaks_because: Multi-mutator GC has N from-nurseries (+ possibly a shared tenured space): contains_polyword, the from_objects pre-pass, the untracked-pointer invariant check, replace_storage, and the POLYML_GC_AUDIT walk all need to become multi-range or every nursery but one is invisible to forwarding — instant heap corruption. (Primarily the GC agent's area, but every edge listed here consumes these same single-range shapes.)

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-runtime/src/space.rs:119-153 (alloc_or_exit → process::exit(4); try_alloc doc: 'Exhaustion is TERMINAL BY DESIGN — callers must NOT try GC-then-retry')
assumptions.what: Alloc-space exhaustion is a terminal user condition, and the no-GC-retry doctrine exists because RTS helpers (do_alloc_ref, ALLOC_WORD_MEMORY) cache the allocation pointer across the call.
assumptions.breaks_because: With small per-thread nurseries, exhaustion becomes the ROUTINE event that must trigger nursery refill or GC — the doctrine inverts. A naive 'GC on full and retry' reintroduces the exact banned pointer-caching corruption; the sound port is refill-with-a-FRESH-nursery (old nursery stays live and unmoved until the next STW GC), and process-exit only on genuine global exhaustion decided at the STW collection.

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-runtime/src/interpreter/mod.rs:1256/1311/1368 (diag: None; children always None) + main.rs:585-586/764-769 (--profile wires only the main interpreter) + diag.rs:14-31 (DiagState per-Interpreter)
assumptions.what: Diagnostics/profiling state is per-Interpreter, enabled only on the CLI's main interpreter; run_until picks the instrumented monomorphisation from ITS OWN diag (mod.rs:4314).
assumptions.breaks_because: With N mutators, --profile silently profiles one thread and reports a steps count that is one thread's share — misleading rather than unsound. Needs per-thread DiagState merged at exit (HashMap merge is trivial) plus aggregated step totals, or an explicit 'main thread only' label.

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-runtime/src/session.rs:226-245 (Session::run wait_for_children only on terminal outcome) + 129-138 (field drop order: interp then _loaded)
assumptions.what: The LoadedImage outlives all execution because the single interpreter is the only referencer, and children (if any) are drained before return on terminal outcomes.
assumptions.breaks_because: On StepBudgetExhausted the Session returns with forked children still RUNNING; if the embedder drops the Session, LoadedImage's spaces (image code/mutable — every thread's code_start points there) are freed under live mutators → UAF. Session::drop must join (or request-kill and join) children; SessionConfig.stack_words is also not inherited by children (for_child_thread hard-codes 1024*1024 at mod.rs:1330).

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-bin/src/main.rs:64-70+577-583 and session.rs:205-210 (untrusted space wiring), main.rs:562-568 (one heap, one image_mutable_root registration)
assumptions.what: The CLI/Session construct exactly one interpreter, one heap in the Runtime, and wire untrusted spaces into that one interpreter.
assumptions.breaks_because: Everything constructed here splits under multi-mutator: heap → shared tenured + per-thread nurseries created at thread registration; untrusted posture + image ranges → shared Runtime state inherited by every child; the printed 'Executed N steps' → an aggregate. The image-mutable GC root is already correctly mirrored into the shared Runtime (mod.rs:1663-1670 / sched.rs:353-359) — the one edge that is ALREADY multi-thread-shaped.

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-runtime/src/rts.rs:7537-7547 (test helper rts_spaces_of builds a single-space RtsSafeSpaces) and tests/malicious-corpus + untrusted_corpus.rs fences
assumptions.what: The untrusted-mode test fixtures and the lint-image-deref surface guard assume the range-set shape 'image spaces + one alloc range'.
assumptions.breaks_because: After the nursery split the fences keep passing while validating the WRONG shape (they never construct multi-nursery sets), so a missed nursery in a range set would not be caught — the regression fence itself needs a multi-nursery negative test.

required_changes: Pluralize the live-space set at the ONE source of truth: replace Interpreter::alloc_space_range() (mod.rs:1719) with a runtime-owned live-space TABLE (image spaces + every registered nursery + shared/tenured space), and make safe_deref::SafeSpaces::validate_obj, RtsSafeSpaces construction (mod.rs:1761), gc::Collector::contains_polyword, the GC audit, and export's ptr_ok/space_end_of all consume it. RtsSafeSpaces' Vec<(usize,usize)> shape already fits; only the construction and invalidation change.

Add a GC-generation (epoch) counter bumped by every collection, stamped into every RtsSafeSpaces snapshot; any consumer that crosses a potential-park/GC point must re-derive (make the check debug_assert + auto-rebuild on mismatch). This turns the currently hand-managed 'pre-park snapshot is stale' discipline (rts.rs:2632) into a mechanism.

Move trust posture (untrusted flag + image SafeSpaces) into the shared Runtime and inherit it in for_child_thread (mod.rs:1370-1376) — the current hard-coded trusted child is a soundness hole for --untrusted + fork.

Route ALL allocation through a per-thread nursery handle: alloc_space_mut/jit_alloc_space_mut (mod.rs:1491-1503) and RtsContext.alloc_space (rts.rs:146, ~45 use sites) point at the calling thread's nursery; retire heap_mut()'s &mut-from-UnsafeCell for mutator-path use (keep it only for the STW collector).

Invert the exhaustion doctrine at the nursery level: try_alloc(None) on a nursery → refill with a FRESH nursery (never move existing objects on the alloc path, preserving the no-GC-retry invariant that protects do_alloc_ref/ALLOC_WORD_MEMORY's cached pointers); alloc_or_exit's process::exit and InterpError::HeapExhausted become the STW-GC-decided global-exhaustion outcome only.

Rework the run_until loop: per-step check reads MY nursery's used/trigger (thread-local, no shared read); the 65536-step poll becomes the GC-stop check on a genuine AtomicBool with release(publish roots)/acquire(collector reads) pairing — replacing mutex-backed gc_requested_relaxed (sched.rs:881) — and cooperative_yield is deleted; define interrupt routing (broadcast or console-thread) instead of first-poller-consumes take_interrupt; make FINISH_REQUESTED a broadcast halt.

Add the GC-stop poll to every loop that steps outside run_until: jit_dispatch_closure_call's nested loop (jit_bridge.rs:522), the CLI POLY_CHECKPOINT_EVERY loop (main.rs:681 — also give it the acquire/release bracket), and the whole-region back-edge poll (either an extra ExnCtx stop-flag field, or the remote-trigger-slam: the runtime sets the region's per-thread trigger to 0 so the next poll takes region_safepoint, which then parks).

Whole-region ExnCtx: gc_used_ptr must target the calling thread's own nursery counter at a stable Boxed address (single writer, no shared plain loads); keep the ExnCtxC layout assertion test (mod.rs:8685) in sync; verify the flag-off path still compiles and stays byte-identical (the ctx fields default to inert i64::MAX trigger).

Make export/PolyExport (rts.rs:1919, export.rs:72) a stop-the-world operation: enter the same barrier as GC (all peers parked with roots published) for the duration of snapshot_gated; hand the walker the full nursery set; in untrusted mode consider making an out-of-range pointer a hard error (or at least a counted warning) instead of a silent Tagged(0) placeholder.

Per-thread diagnostics: give each ThreadContext its own DiagState and merge on take_diagnostics (or explicitly document/print 'main thread only'); aggregate per-thread step counts for the CLI report; POLY_CHECKPOINT_EVERY reporting becomes per-thread-tagged.

Session lifecycle: join children in Session::drop (not just on terminal run outcomes); propagate SessionConfig.stack_words (and heap/nursery sizing policy) to for_child_thread; document that _loaded must outlive every mutator.

Extend the untrusted fences: a multi-nursery negative test in untrusted_corpus (a valid pointer into nursery #2 must validate; a stale range-set must be rejected by the epoch check), and update tools/lint-image-deref.py's surface model if the validator entry points change shape.
hazards: The validator-UAF inversion: a stale RtsSafeSpaces alloc range doesn't just mis-answer — validate_obj_fit (rts.rs:417) and SafeSpaces::validate_obj (safe_deref.rs:320) DEREF p.sub(1) once the range check passes, so a range covering a freed from-space Box turns the SAFETY mechanism into the UAF. Any port that keeps pre-call snapshots must prove no GC can intervene, or gate every post-park use on the epoch.

Validating pointers into a PEER's live nursery reads headers the peer may be mid-writing (alloc = bump, then set_length_word, then body writes — non-atomic). Even 'read-only' membership+header checks become racy; untrusted validation of cross-nursery pointers is only sound against bounds frozen at a safepoint (use each nursery's last-published used-mark, not live capacity).

Naive 'nursery full → GC → retry alloc' reintroduces the exact banned heap-corruption class (space.rs:131-142): do_alloc_ref and ALLOC_WORD_MEMORY cache the allocation pointer across the call, and moving objects on the alloc path invalidates them. Refill-with-fresh-nursery (old nursery immortal until next STW) is the only shape that preserves the invariant without re-auditing every RTS allocator.

gc_requested_relaxed is mutex-backed; converting it to a bare AtomicBool silently loses the happens-before edges the H1/H2 root-publication discipline currently gets from the sched mutex (roots must be VISIBLE to the collector before 'parked' is). The atomic port needs explicit release-on-publish / acquire-on-collect ordering, and the concurrency_gc_handshake tests re-run under TSan-like scrutiny.

for_child_thread's trusted-child default means the first fork in an --untrusted session already de-fangs the safe mode TODAY (real-threads+untrusted combo); a multi-mutator port that copies this constructor propagates the hole to the mainline path.

Loops that bypass run_until (JIT trampoline nested step loop, CLI checkpoint loop, any test driving step() directly) deadlock the STW barrier — and the CLI checkpoint loop doesn't even hold ML memory. These are invisible in single-mutator testing and only fail under load; grep for direct step() call sites before flipping the flag.

JIT'd native frames (per-function JIT) hold heap-pointer bits in Cranelift registers/spills across trampoline calls; more mutators = more GCs = the latent unscanned-native-frame UAF class goes from theoretical to hot. Either forbid GC across per-function-JIT trampolines (pre-reserve nursery space) or keep --jit disabled in multi-mutator mode initially.

ExnCtx.gc_used_ptr dangles if per-thread nursery structs live in a growable container (Vec push on thread registration reallocates); nurseries must be individually Boxed/Arc'd with stable addresses. The ctx is built once per region dispatch (mod.rs:8117) and a nursery SWAP (refill) mid-region also invalidates it — the refill path must either update the live ctx or only swap at region safepoints.

Untrusted export's silent Tagged(0) placeholder for out-of-range pointers (export.rs:369-371) means a missing nursery range yields a syntactically valid but semantically corrupt image with zero diagnostics — the failure is only discovered when the exported image misbehaves later.

FINISH_REQUESTED and take_interrupt are consume-and-clear globals: with N pollers, delivery target is a race. PolyFinish half-halting the process (one thread returns, peers run on) is a hang-shaped bug, not a crash-shaped one.

The alloc_space_range 'capacity end, not used end' choice (mod.rs:1732-1737) exists so a just-allocated object validates; with per-thread nurseries the same choice makes a peer's UNALLOCATED nursery tail 'valid' territory for forged pointers in untrusted mode — validation against peers should use published used-marks while validation against one's own nursery keeps the capacity bound.

Byte-identical regression fences (the 228-entry RTS fingerprint, diff-oracle, self-bootstrap) only pin the single-threaded flag-OFF path; every multi-mutator change must be flag-gated exactly like POLY_REAL_THREADS or the fences go blind to the mainline they were built to protect.
maps.design_opinion: Make the Runtime own a single authoritative LIVE-SPACE TABLE (image spaces + shared/tenured space + one stably-addressed nursery per registered thread) with a GC epoch counter, and have every edge consumer — safe_deref, RtsSafeSpaces, export's ptr_ok, the GC's contains/audit — read or snapshot THAT, with epoch-stamped snapshots that self-invalidate instead of the current hand-managed per-site staleness discipline. Route all allocation through a per-thread nursery handle (thread-local trigger check, single-writer used counter) and adopt refill-with-fresh-nursery on exhaustion so the load-bearing no-GC-retry invariant survives without re-auditing 45 RTS alloc sites; nursery memory is only reclaimed/moved at STW collections. Treat export, and initially anything untrusted-mode, as STW operations — they are cold paths and the barrier already exists; do not try to make the typed-deref predicate concurrent-reader-safe in v1. Fix the two edge soundness holes that exist even before the port (trusted forked children under --untrusted; the checkpoint/JIT step loops that never poll) since they are prerequisites, not new work. Keep --jit and --whole-region behind their flags in multi-mutator mode until the native-frame root question and the region stop-flag are settled — the remote-trigger-slam (set a region's per-thread trigger to 0 to force its next back-edge poll into the parking slow path) is the cheapest sound region-stop mechanism. Above all, gate everything so the single-threaded default stays byte-identical; the existing fences are only meaningful for the path they actually execute.

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-runtime/src/rts.rs:1853-1905 (FINISH_REQUESTED) + interpreter/mod.rs:4686-4688 (consume at top of EVERY step) + mod.rs:4391-4433 (wait_for_children)
assumptions.what: PolyFinish/PolyTerminate assume the next thread to execute a step IS the program: a process-global flag, consumed-and-cleared by whichever interpreter steps first.
assumptions.breaks_because: With N mutators the consumer can be a WORKER CHILD: OS.Process.exit called by the REPL can be consumed by a child's step_impl, which returns Returned(code) into child_thread_main — i.e. the exit KILLS A RANDOM WORKER while the exiting thread and all peers continue; parked/blocked threads never observe the flag at all; wait_for_children then waits indefinitely for the survivors. This is a semantic INVERSION, sharper than the maps' 'one thread halts, peers keep running'. Also process-global: one embedded Session's exit halts an unrelated Session (the test-only FINISH_FLAG_LOCK at rts.rs:6524-6525 exists precisely because parallel cargo tests already collide on it — the canary for the whole globals class).

assumptions.what: CommandLine.arguments and the --use synthetic-stdin queue are process statics, assumed owned by 'the one run'.
assumptions.breaks_because: The synthetic queue is Mutex-safe per read, but read-synthetic-then-fallthrough-to-real-stdin (rts.rs:6244-6251, 6358-6363) is not atomic across two fd-0 readers: thread A can drain the last synthetic bytes while thread B falls through to real stdin, delivering real input BEFORE the synthesized `Bootstrap.use` completes — declaration interleaving/ordering inversion. Two Sessions in one process stomp each other's args/queue/finish state. All of this must move into Runtime (none of the four maps lists these statics; the promised shared-state map was never delivered).

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-runtime/src/interrupt.rs:17-45 + main.rs:22-43 (handler) + mod.rs:4650-4659 (take_interrupt at the poll) vs mod.rs:2424-2434 (the existing PolyThreadBroadcastInterrupt machinery)
assumptions.what: SIGINT is one process-global AtomicBool consumed by the first poller; clear_interrupt may be called when a REPL returns to its prompt.
assumptions.breaks_because: Beyond the maps' 'random thread' note: (a) a compute-bound CHILD that polls first raises SML Interrupt in ITSELF — Ctrl-C kills a worker instead of interrupting the REPL; (b) clear_interrupt at one thread's prompt swallows an interrupt aimed at a peer; (c) the CORRECT sink already exists in-tree — the broadcast-interrupt path honoring per-thread PFLAG_BROADCAST/INTMASK (mod.rs:2424, port of upstream BroadcastInterrupt) — SIGINT should feed it rather than the raw flag. No map proposes wiring SIGINT to the existing machinery.

assumptions.what: The basis signal thread parks forever in an infinite block_on_event loop; being a daemon (skipped by wait_for_children) is assumed sufficient shutdown semantics.
assumptions.breaks_because: The loop never checks handle.requests, so it is immune to KILL — any broadcast-KILL shutdown protocol (the required fix for PolyFinish/halt semantics) hangs on it forever. Worse for N mutators: block_gen is bumped by EVERY mutex-unlock/condvar-wake/exit process-wide, so the daemon wakes on every such event, performs a full make_send_roots capture (Box + root capture) + publish + reacquire + free per event — a per-event scheduler-lock tax that scales with total mutex traffic across all mutators.

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-runtime/src/sched.rs:197 (NEXT_THREAD_ID dense from 1, per-process) + mod.rs:2602-2610 and mod.rs:7561-7574 (tagged handle id written into thread-object word 0) + rts.rs:1919 (poly_export walks the live heap)
assumptions.what: Thread identity (the tagged id in thread-object word 0 that Thread.interrupt/kill/isActive resolve via handle_for_thread_object, mod.rs:2028) is assumed process-lifetime-only.
assumptions.breaks_because: Exported images / warm checkpoints PERSIST thread objects. A checkpoint exported from a real-threads run bakes live ids (1,2,3…); a fresh process restarts NEXT_THREAD_ID at 1, so a resurrected image thread object COLLIDES with a fresh live handle — Thread.interrupt/kill on the stale object targets an unrelated live thread. Latent today only because default-mode stubs write Tagged(0) (rts.rs:5165; mod.rs:7546-7550 leaves word 0 zero unless real_threads_enabled) — it goes live the moment the giant lock breaks and real threads are mainline. Upstream's raw threadRef pointer cannot collide by counting; our dense ids WILL. No map connects thread identity to the export/checkpoint story.

assumptions.what: Step budgets and error halts are per-thread: --max-steps / Session budgets bound only the calling thread; children run with an effectively infinite cap.
assumptions.breaks_because: A looping child is unkillable-by-budget, and a main-thread TERMINAL halt (HeapExhausted, BadImage, budget-with-terminal-intent) leaves children running while wait_for_children blocks the CLI/Session forever — there is no 'root halted ⇒ kill children' rule anywhere. Under N mutators, runaway children become the common failure mode of every negative test. The maps cover PolyFinish but not the budget/error-halt asymmetry.

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-runtime/src/interpreter/mod.rs:4248-4255 (pub fn run) + mod.rs:4261-4263 (pub fn step = bare step_impl::<true>) + session.rs:250-257 (interpreter_mut escape hatch)
assumptions.what: The PUBLIC single-step API assumes the caller doesn't need the scheduler: step()/run() have neither the acquire_running bracket nor the 65536-step safepoint poll.
assumptions.breaks_because: Map 4 caught two instances (the CLI POLY_CHECKPOINT_EVERY loop at main.rs:681-697, the JIT trampoline loop at jit_bridge.rs:522-569) but missed that the API ITSELF is the general hole: any embedder or test driving step()/run() (reachable via Session::interpreter_mut) executes bytecode without in_ml and never parks for a peer's gc_requested — heap mutation outside the handshake (UAF) plus a stalled STW barrier. Must be bracketed or deprecated in multi-mutator mode.

assumptions.what: The concurrency MODE itself is a process-global, read-once memoized static.
assumptions.breaks_because: A multi-mutator mode flag scoped like this cannot differ per Runtime/Session and freezes at first read; the registry_len()-instant stub fallbacks (mod.rs:2210/2263/2306) already have the TOCTOU map 3 noted, but the memoized static adds a second freeze axis. Mode must become Runtime-construction state.

assumptions.what: A native-side slot documented as 'the thread's exit result word (valid once exited)'.
assumptions.breaks_because: It is never written today (always Tagged(0)) and is NOT part of any GC root walk (ThreadRoots/ForkRoots never visit it). The first future change that stores a heap pointer there (result-carrying join/exit) creates a GC-invisible root — a UAF that no audit covers. Either delete the field or wire it into the handle's forwarded roots now.

assumptions.site: crates/polyml-runtime/src/interpreter/mod.rs:2209+ (try_thread_rts intercepts thread RTS calls BY NAME before table dispatch) + the frozen 228-entry register() order
assumptions.what: Checkpoint compatibility rests on two distinct anchors: register() ORDER (dispatch tokens) and entry-point NAMES (the interception layer).
assumptions.breaks_because: Good news the maps missed: because thread-RTS semantics are name-intercepted at dispatch, breaking the giant lock needs NO token-order change — warm checkpoints stay valid. But the flip side: entry-point NAMES are now load-bearing for images (a rename silently de-intercepts, falling through to the single-thread stub — e.g. MutexBlock would quietly become reset-and-return under N mutators, destroying mutual exclusion with no error), and any NEW nursery/scheduler RTS entries must be APPENDED (the fingerprint test pins 228).

required_changes: RE-BASELINE THE PLAN AGAINST HEAD: the maps' 'still-unparked blocking calls (recv/send/stdin/OS.Process.system)' claims are STALE (they mirror pre-stage-3 CLAUDE.md). Commits b2beb6a + a269e51 already park: OS.Process.system's child-wait (rts.rs:5895), send/sendto via native copy-out (rts.rs:2560/2736), recv/recvfrom via bounce buffer + rts_park_root cell (rts.rs:2630/2812), write_array (rts.rs:5288), stdin readArray/readVec (rts.rs:6275/6357), accept/connect/select (rts.rs:2387/2431/3226). Re-scope map 3's required-change #11 to what is actually left: generalize rts_park_root from ONE cell to a small root list, and mechanize the stale-pre-park RtsSafeSpaces re-derive (epoch stamps) — do NOT re-implement parking.

Define multi-thread exit semantics as a unit: PolyFinish/PolyTerminate become Runtime-scoped state that triggers a KILL broadcast to every registered handle + a bounded join + a process-level exit code, instead of a consumed-on-read global; wait_for_children must honor a pending finish; and the PolyWaitForSignal daemon loop (mod.rs:2503-2505) must check handle.requests on each wake so KILL can actually terminate it.

Route SIGINT through the EXISTING broadcast-interrupt machinery (mod.rs:2424, per-thread PFLAG_BROADCAST/INTMASK) instead of the first-poller-consumes flag; give clear_interrupt defined per-thread semantics (a REPL prompt clearing a peer's pending interrupt is wrong today).

Migrate the rts.rs process statics into Runtime (or a RuntimeGlobals owned by it): COMMAND_ARGS, SYNTHETIC_STDIN, FINISH_REQUESTED, INTERRUPT_PENDING routing state, and decide DIR_STATE (rts.rs:5349) scoping — prerequisite for both N mutators and N Sessions; make the synthetic-stdin drain + real-stdin fallthrough atomic per read request.

Make thread identity export-safe BEFORE real threads are mainline: scrub thread-object word 0 (the tagged handle id) in poly_export/snapshot (or on load), or namespace ids with a per-image epoch — otherwise reloaded checkpoints alias stale ids onto fresh live handles and Thread.interrupt/kill mis-target (sched.rs:197 + mod.rs:2602-2610 + export.rs).

Add a root-terminal policy: when the root thread halts (Returned / Err / finish), broadcast KILL, join with a bound, then exit — covering the budget/error-halt asymmetry (children run at u64::MAX, mod.rs:2780) that otherwise hangs wait_for_children on any runaway child.

Bracket or deprecate the public step()/run() API (mod.rs:4248-4263) in multi-mutator mode — acquire_running + the safepoint poll — not just the two loops map 4 found; audit all direct step() call sites (tests included).

Make the concurrency mode Runtime-construction state instead of the memoized POLY_REAL_THREADS static (mod.rs:343-350), and convert the registry_len()-instant stub gates to a monotonic per-Runtime mode as map 3 proposed.
Either delete ThreadHandle.result (sched.rs:137, never written) or add it to the handle's GC-forwarded roots — close the landmine before someone implements result-carrying join.

Commission the MISSING FIFTH MAP (shared-state/memory-model): only 4 of the 5 promised recon maps were delivered; nobody has enumerated (a) the rts.rs statics inventory (partially done here) or (b) the full plain-PolyWord LOAD/STORE opcode surface (every STORE_W/LOAD/byte-vector/ref-assignment handler) that needs an atomic-view policy — map 3 covers it in one hazard line, with no site list. This enumeration is prerequisite to declaring the port non-UB.

Add negative-control tests for the gap class: (1) OS.Process.exit with running children — assert process exits and no child survives; (2) SIGINT with a compute child — assert the REPL (not the worker) receives Interrupt; (3) export-under-real-threads → reload → interrupt the resurrected thread object — assert no cross-kill of a fresh thread; (4) two concurrent fd-0 readers against a synthetic-stdin queue — assert ordering; (5) KILL delivered to the parked signal daemon — assert it exits.

Keep the name-based try_thread_rts interception layer stable across the rewrite (entry-point NAMES are baked into images); add a test asserting every intercepted name still matches, alongside the 228-entry token fingerprint.
hazards: MAPS ARE STALE ON PARKING COVERAGE (map 3's RtsPark assumption + its 'unparked blockers' hazard; map 4's design-opinion premise; CLAUDE.md's Open Issues): recv/send/stdin-read/stdout-write/OS.Process.system ALL park now (rts.rs:2560, 2630, 2736, 2812, 5288, 5895, 6275, 6357; commits b2beb6a, a269e51). A team executing the maps verbatim re-implements finished work and mis-prioritizes; the LIVE residuals are the single rts_park_root cell, the hand-managed stale-snapshot discipline, and the step loops that never poll.

MAP 3/4 UNDERSTATE PolyFinish: verified at mod.rs:4686-4688, the consumer is whichever thread steps next — including a CHILD, whose run_until returns Returned(code) into child_thread_main as an ordinary thread exit. OS.Process.exit can therefore kill a random worker while the exiting thread continues; it is an inversion, not just a partial halt, and wait_for_children then blocks on the survivors.

MAP 3's mutex-reset fix has a mis-application trap (verified against upstream): the EXTRA plain write is real in the PolyThreadMutexUnlock arm (mod.rs:2241-2247; upstream Processes::MutexUnlock processes.cpp:441-460 does NOT write the word) — but the CondVarWait/WaitUntil preamble resets (mod.rs:2267/2313) ARE upstream-faithful (AtomicallyReleaseMutex → InterpreterReleaseMutex, bytecode.cpp:2465-2471, writes TAGGED(0) under mutexLock). 'Remove the reset' applied uniformly would break CondVar; only the Unlock arm's write goes, the preamble's becomes an atomic swap.

Spot-checks CONFIRMING the maps (for the record): gc_requested_relaxed really takes the scheduler Mutex (sched.rs:881-883); for_child_thread really hard-codes trusted + default SafeSpaces (mod.rs:1371-1376); the CLI checkpoint loop really drives bare step() with no acquire bracket (main.rs:681-697; step() = step_impl at mod.rs:4261); gc() really scans self.image_mutable_roots.clone() not Runtime.image_roots (mod.rs:2935); try_alloc really is a plain non-atomic RMW (space.rs:143-153). The maps' core allocation/GC/scheduler analysis is sound.

THREAD-ID RESURRECTION: dense per-process ids (sched.rs:197) persisted in exported thread objects collide with fresh handles on reload — handle_for_thread_object (mod.rs:2028) then resolves a stale image object to a LIVE unrelated thread. Invisible in all current tests because default-mode exports bake Tagged(0); becomes real exactly when the mission ships.

THE UN-KILLABLE DAEMON: PolyWaitForSignal's loop { block_on_event() } (mod.rs:2503-2505) never checks requests — every shutdown design that broadcasts KILL (needed for PolyFinish, root-halt, Session teardown) deadlocks on it unless the loop is fixed first; it also does a full root-capture/publish cycle on EVERY global block event, a hidden N-mutator scheduler tax.

FINISH_FLAG_LOCK (rts.rs:6524-6525) is the smoking gun that the process-globals problem already bites: a test-only mutex serializing cargo-test workers around FINISH_REQUESTED. Any global that needed a test lock under parallel TESTS will need Runtime scoping under parallel MUTATORS.

The missing fifth map (shared-state/memory-model) means the plain-PolyWord LOAD/STORE opcode surface — every SML ref/array/mutable-record store racing between mutators, formally UB in Rust — has NO site enumeration anywhere; map 3's single hazard line is the only coverage. Do not let the scheduler rewrite start before that inventory exists.

Session/embedding residue beyond map 4: Session has NO Drop joining children (verified — only run() on terminal outcomes calls wait_for_children), and interpreter_mut() hands embedders the unbracketed step()/run() API; two Sessions in one process are silently unsupported (shared FINISH/args/stdin globals) — should be documented or enforced until the globals migrate.
gaps.design_opinion: Fix the semantic layer before the scheduler surgery: exit (PolyFinish → broadcast KILL + bounded join), SIGINT (feed the existing BroadcastInterrupt machinery, not the first-poller flag), the killable signal-daemon loop, and the process-globals → Runtime migration are all cheap, flag-invisible, and every multi-mutator test you will write depends on deterministic shutdown — build them first or the negative-control suite itself hangs. Second, re-baseline the four maps against HEAD: their allocation/GC/scheduler core is verified sound, but the blocking-syscall parking they treat as future work is already done (stage 3, commits b2beb6a/a269e51), so the real remaining park work is the root-cell list and epoch-stamped space snapshots. Third, treat checkpoints as first-class in the design: the name-based try_thread_rts interception is what lets warm images survive the transition with zero token churn (keep it stable and fence it), and thread-object identity must be export-scrubbed before real threads are mainline or reloaded images will mis-target live threads. Finally, commission the missing shared-state/memory-model map — the plain heap LOAD/STORE surface is the one area with no site enumeration, and it is precisely the area where 'works on x86' hides UB that Rust's model does not forgive.

_note: 5th map (shared-state/memory-model) folded into docs/parallel-design.md prose; it re-ran standalone after an API timeout in the fleet.
